user_comment: My teen daughter is 5’3” and 170 pounds. She’s been looking for an oversized jean jacket for awhile. We decided to take a chance on this one. She got the medium light blue. She loves the style and fit. My husband who is 5’10” and 200 pounds tried it on as a joke and it actually fit him too, just not oversized.

My teen daughter is 5’4”, 118 lbs, and has 27.5” waist. She wears size small or 4 pants. She got these jeans in size medium and she’s happy with the fit. The waist is snug but it is not too tight. The hips and legs have a wide relaxed cut. The pants are a bit too long for her to wear with flat shoes so she usually folds them up unless she’s wearing heels, platforms, or bulkier sneakers. There is no fabric content tag. The fabric feels like a quality heavier type of denim with a very small spandex addition. These jeans have a little give but they are not stretchy. The stitching is nice on the exterior and has some small issues on the interior but none are bothersome and don’t compromise the durability of stitching. There was also a piece of plastic foil stitched under the exterior waistband, perhaps a leftover of some kind of a tag but I was able to rip it out.

My teen daughter is 5’5” and weighs approximately 115 lbs. She usually wears size small pants. These cargo sweatpants in size small fit her well. They are the right length for my daughter’s height and the stretchy elastic waistband is comfortable and well fitting. The waistband has interior drawstrings. On my pictures the drawstrings are sticking out but I assure you these are interior drawstrings. Overall, my daughter likes the fit but based on the pictures in the listing she expected the pant legs from the knee down to be wider than they are. The fabric is warm and cozy. It is a 65% cotton 35% polyester blend. The workmanship is good.

My teen daughter weighs 115 lbs and she’s almost 5’6” tall. She wears shorts in size small or 4. I ordered these shorts in color 21148 size small for her and they are a perfect fit. The fabric content is 85% cotton 15% polyester. The shorts look exactly as they do in the pictures in the listing. There’s a lot of distressing and rips on the front. Underneath most of those rips there are white interior pockets that show through the rips where the shorts are being worn. On the back the rips are only on the back pockets. As you can see on my picture there was some uneven fringe distressing done on the back of the legs openings. My daughter trimmed the too long strings with scissors after taking the picture. The shorts are more skin revealing on the front but they have a full coverage on the back. They are high waisted. The stitching is ok overall but there was one spot in the front with missing stitch, and a few others will loose threads. I’m taking a star off for those sewing imperfections but my daughter really likes the fit and the look of these denim shorts and she wears them often.

My teenage daughter said this style of pants is stylish right now so I'm giving them a try. For reference, I am 4'10" tall and 123lbs and I ordered a size small, which fits pretty accurately (I'll need to hem them, but that's common for me). The style is 'barrel' so they are wide-leg and with the thicker material (almost denim?) they maintain the width. For a petite frame, it kinda makes me look shorter and that's why I can't decide if I like them? That said, the pants are very comfortable. I love the high-waist with button + zipper + cinch tie. The design and pattern is cute. My daughter said she likes them on me (and she's a critic), I haven't decided yet. If you are of average height and like a wide-leg jean, this could work great for you! True to size.

My teenager was not up to taking a pic for me so I grabbed a screenshot from our nest camera :) These are really really cute and the price is great for what you’re getting. These are a Medium - my daughter is 5’ and approximately 125 pounds. They are a little long so we fixed that by folding them twice. They actually look great cuffed and add to the look. Made with stretchy material so they're easier to move around in than your standard denim overalls. Big fan of these!

In the first photo the right leg is rolled under. They were also not flattering at all. Definitely not for an Apple shape body. I’ve tried other wide leg jeans that fit much better than these.

My waist measurement (smallest point where I might wear a belt) is 31", which is at the top of the small size and low in the medium size, so I ordered both to compare. The small size is about 38" total length (not counting the buckle), and the medium is 43" inches long. The small belt definitely fits my small waist measure; I would be using a hole in the middle of the range. However, it would be way too small to fit lower on my hips, where I sometimes wear a belt (my hip measure increases to 41" ). The medium size belt is a touch too large for my 31" waist (shown in the photo) -- I will have to punch another hole. However, I will keep this one so that I can wear it lower on my hips as well. So my advice for sizing is to take into account where you wear your belt, and if you're near the ends of the measurement ranges, size up for versatility! Too big is better than too small since you can punch another hole, or I guess shorten the whole belt by removing the buckle.

My wife is disappointed in the Lee Women’s Sculpting Slim Fit Slim Leg Pull On Jean. They simply don't live up to the "sculpting slim fit” description. If anything, they do the opposite. They are loose around the abdomen and crotch and the size 8 is way too long on her 5'5 ½" frame. She has to wear heels to keep the hem from dragging. The back fits okay but it's not overly flattering or special in any way. The sizing seems way off. 8 medium is more like a 12 large. No thank you to vanity sizing in this instance. The material is smooth but not breathable. The stitching is decent in some areas but seems messy in other spots -– definitely not refined like lux jeans, so you're getting what you pay for. The overall style is unflattering and makes my wife feel weighed down and uncomfortable. These are jeans she probably won't wear again — she flat out dislikes them on many levels. I would agree that they do not bring out the best in her form and style and I noticed she had to keep pulling them up while we were shopping. ~ Kort

Normally when I buy jeans, I spend 20-30 minutes crying in front of the mirror in a self loathing kind of way. I’ve suffered from BDD since I can remember and pants, jeans especially, have always been my biggest nemesis. All that being said… sweet baby Jesus these jeans are the freaking epitome of comfort. For reference, I’m 5’4”, 130lbs and haven’t seen the inside of a gym in probably 10 years and my ass is clearly nonexistent — I purchased the Maui Views (Waterless) in a size (4) 27 Short. Unpopular opinion but I detest high waisted jeans. I’m “short torso - long leg” so hard pass on anything that even gets near my belly button. I was nervous about the rise of these but am so glad I took the chance because they truly sit just perfectly under my belly button and at my waist. I’ve never felt so comfortable and honestly, like myself, in a pair of jeans. I could not recommend a pair more than these. *only hiccup in my purchase was that the original pair claimed to have been delivered but was in fact not. Amazon customer service quickly rectified that and sent a replacement pair overnight.

Not a huge fan of these pants. 1. They are not what you would consider linen. When you think of linen you think of a thin, "flowy", wrinkly, lightweight, and breathable summer pant. These are none of these things...they are more like rather stiff chinos with texture. The description pictures definitely paints the picture of linen but it is deceiving and misleading in my opinion. 2. The pants went all the way down to the ground when I thought they would be above the ankles per the description picture (again, very misleading...this model must have been 5'11" or taller!!). I had to roll them 4 times to get them "cropped" looking and then they just looked weird. I am 5'6" and 190 pounds so I am not super short and I got size Large. I expected more for pants that are $35. I will wear them to work but they won't be my go to summer beach pant sadly :(

NOTE: I don't typically wear shirts tucked in, but for this review I felt I needed to show the fit better. Don't judge too harshly! :) Size Reference: Ordered XXL. I am 5'5", 180 pounds, a 35" waist and 46.5" hips. Weight is carried in my hips and rather large backside. I highly recommend checking the size picture before ordering. I ordered the XXL according to my largest measurement, my hip size. I always find I have a waist gap anyway, so I expected a bit of gap here. Lately I have been struggling more to find pants that fit my hips without highlighting every single fat globule under my skin. If I had realized how stretchy the denim was, I would have ordered an XL. There is so much stretch at the front right at the belly area that it just gathers there. When I sit down I have a big denim poof sticking up. The back has the expected gap, but the thighs fit nicely. There are no belt loops, so there is no cinching the waist in like that. I will likely sew in some darts at the back to help with that. The stretch is so stretchy that I don't even have to unbutton these to take my pants off. I just pulled them right off, no problems. Hopefully giving my measurements and showing pictures of my body proportion will help you as you look to order the right size. I love how soft and stretchy they are, but I definitely needed a smaller size!

I’m very, very happy with these jeans, I skipped and moonwalked down the hallway!!

OK here’s the real deal on this shirt, after reading multiple reviews. I am 5‘7“ and 125 pounds. Someone else my weight/height bought an XS and said it was perfect, and you can see in her pic, her sleeves are too short. I am a true small most of the time (or med.) and this shirt is the right fit in my size. I wanted something more fitted, this shirt has princess seams on the back. Yes it smells a little funny, so wash it! The mid blue is the perfect color for a slightly faded vintage look. The fabric isn’t true denim which it shouldn’t be as a shirt, it’s more like a lighter chambray, but it has the look of denim, which is great for me, I didn’t want it stiff. You can wear it open as a shacket, but it won’t provide much warmth. I wanted to wear it as a shirt. It’s 100% cotton so I expect a little shrinkage, but to me, it would fit even better. [Update: washed/dried, it shrank a bit and fits better! Mostly sleeve and shirttail length.] If you want it more oversized, I would suggest going up as size. I love fashion and can’t wait to style this in endless ways! It’s SO the classic that belongs in every woman’s closet, that’s why they call it wrangler retro!. The pocket placement and size is perfect for us bustier girls.

OK so first things first, I feel like some reviews need a but of a reminder that these are inexpensive Amazon shorts. They arent BAD quality, just don't be expecting the nicest of zippers or buttons. I have huge thighs compared to by waist (I wish that looked as good as it sounds but I promise it does not). My thighs will pull up any shorts at all unless I go full out knee length which I am not doing. Finding shorts to fit my thighs is near impossible. This will be my third pair. The stretch and little side slit helps immensely. For size, I got a medium. I'm about 175 5'6" 28" waist 43" hips. I can't see the size chart right now but I think that put me at an xl. After reading the reviews I kept seeing "i sized down and would have been fine doing so again". And if you have big thighs you know you have to err on the side of a little tight or you get that excess bulge of fabric in the middle that's not a good look. These fit super comfortably. As you can see, more on the snug side but not tight. They have plenty of give. If you don't have the spare fabric when you sit issue you can size down just one I think. Kinda wish there were options without lots of fringe that didn't involve cuffs (those REALLY don't work with thighs), because I'm not 13 anymore, but honestly I'm gonna trim it off, replace the buttons, and these will be worn a lot. Overall 10/10, I'm happy.

Ok so I bought these brown heart jeans for a Valentine’s Day show I’m going to and I saw the review another person posted and she had them on and they looked cute on her!!! She also said you could size down which I didn’t. I ordered a medium (I’m 5’8, 135 lbs) which is my normal size in everything and they JUST fit me. I have hips and long legs so the medium is a little tight in the hips (but still wearable) and a little too short in the length as these are boot cut so they’re supposed to hang longer. I actually might have needed a size UP!!! I cut the hem out of the bottom to try to gain some length but they’re still slightly too short for my liking. The other thing that pissed me off is that I bought them for $19.99 and then the next day they were $14.99. I’d say they’re worth $14.99 but had I tried them on in a store I wouldn’t have bought them for $20.

Ok so I’ve been wanting some overalls for a while now and I finally took the leap! I had a pair from H&M a few years ago and I loved how they looked but I absolutely hated how they fit. On top of that, they have tons and TONS of POCKETS!!! DEEP POCKETS LADIES, YOU HEARD THAT RIGHT!!!!!!! I’m talking about FIT YOUR ENTIRE PHONE in ‘em pockets!!! I could pass away. I usually wear a 6 in jeans so I got a medium and I will say they are a little loose, but I like that personally for me. If you want them more form fitting, I’d size down. Ok, I ended up buying two different sizes, & since they didn't have the large in the same color, I got it in a lighter blue instead. For reference, I'm apx. 5'7" & 138lbs & have what they say is more of an 'hourglass' figure with an apx. 29-30" waist & apx. 40" hips and the darker blue pants are the Med. & the lighter are the large! The med. fits more like a small in the waist, that's why I decided to try the large. I was thinking it'd be perfect, but it fits more like an XL! Odd to say the least! I can't decide yet which I will be keeping, so thought I'd post this with BOTH so you could see for yourself & hopefully it will help you decide. The colors are oddly close, & pretty close to true in these pics.. This is how they look right out of the package, so yes, they are very wrinkled! I have no doubt though, being as I've dealt with this type of material before, that once washed & hung or dried, they will be fine! Worst case, nothing an iron won't fix! :-) I love the flowyness (if that's even a word LOL) of the pants, but prefer the large for this! Just a little more like a skirt with that size, which is what I like better! Love the flirty aspect of the slit in the front too! It's on both sides, but I just posted the pic of the one. I like the length better on the large too, as the Med. just seems a little TOO short for my taste! But, I actually prefer the darker blue?!!? So I'm torn!! I think I will end up keeping the lighter Large & just take in the elastic some so it'll fit me better & return the Med.! The waist is mostly elastic, except for the very front where it has the tie, so it's on the forgiving side for fit which is nice if you fluctuate in weight like I do! I tend to go up or down 5-10 lbs now & then! LOL Nicely made though, and is priced ok, so I would recommend. They have some nice detail that other pants like this don't which is a huge plus for me! The material is nice & light weight & perfect for spring/summer, but then they could just as easily be paired with nylons for those colder times! Love that they can be either causal or dressy too! Very versatile! I'm always on the lookout for those kinds of buys! Especially now when our dollar isn't going as far! The material has a sort of texture to it to which is kind of nice! Not something that is distracting though! All & all, they are cute, & I like them! LOVE that they come in so many colors too! I just might have to get another couple of these in other colors soon!! Only wished they had a royal blue and/or a hot pink! Maybe they will hear me and add some?! Well....hope this helps! Thanks much! ENJOY!

You're going to love these jeans!!

Ok, well...since there were no reviews, I wasn't sure about these, but thought I'd give them a try! I have to say I was surprised with how comfy they are! VERY Stretchy, so they are VEEEEERY forgiving size wise just wished they were a wee LONGER!! I went with their measurements & this is a Large! Ok... first, I gotta say: these pants are super cute and comfy. I have always had a harder time shopping for pants, because of my extremely curvy figure. Usually, if it fits my hips, it's too big for my waist. These hug me in all the right places. I am 5'7" 270lbs with 55" hips! My shape is in between hourglass and pear. Typically, in stretch pants, I wear a size 2x to 3x depending on the brand. In jeans I wear a size 24. So, I purchased these in 4x. They are just right (provided they don't shrink in the dryer!). I cannot comment on durability, as I only got them today and only tried them on to see if they fit and take pics for your review. If anything changes I'll update. However, right now I do recommend these and now I think I want to get a couple other colors, as well. Update: they did not shrink in the dryer on standard low heat. The only thing that I don't like is the elastic bad twists, however they still seem to lay flat once I get them pulled up. So far I still like them and will probably order other colors in the near future.

Okay so my view of these and how I feel about them. Pairs bought:size 14 black, size 15 black.Material Contents: 98%cotton 2%Spandex each pair. I'm 5'6" 185-190lb on my heavy side rn, w/meas. of 38",33",46". Size 13-14 is my usual size currently. These in sz.14 are what I have on in all 4 pics.The waist fits me alright, the shorts as a whole feel like they suck me up with moderate belly/flab control, and is slightly forgiving with a small gap in the back as pictured. Although they're 98%cotton there is almost no breathability and very little stretch in the fabric and waist. The crotch is on the tighter side for me even though they have a longer 7" inseam, the cut is weird through the groin area and I wouldn't want to wear these in a hot humidity environment. Similar to how others have stated, I can see how these won't hold their shape after wear, they wrinkle easily and would have to be ironed even after the dryer, they flare/bow out at the bottom sides, and they are soft in the inside and outside however any kind of marks show up on this type of material especially since it's a darker shade of black. The front pockets are of decent sizing, can be difficult ti access with the shorts tighter around the hip and waist, and the back pockets exist except they're sewn in of which can be carefully cut out and removed. They are naturally snug around my problem area where my belly pooch is however this isn't the case with my other Lee shorts sz14. These are a nice casual short that I'd wear to a business-casual event outside or preferably inside where extrenuous movement wouldn't be involved although these would be my last choice if nothing else was available--this type of short is either for a unique body type, someone who doesn't mind the flaws, or the flaws work for them. Returning and on the hunt again.Happy Shopping y'all 😀

OMG! Finally a cool and comfy blue jeans pants! I have got two white jeans pairs from Amazon, I love and one blue, which I don't like too much. These jeans are amazing though! OMG! I love these! I can NEVER find comfortable jeans that fit well. These are a good stretchy high waist pants. I'm 5'8 and 205 lbs so I'm kinda tall and chubs but these FIT yaaaay. Omg… I love these wranglers. I am 5’1 and wear a 20wX30 short and I have never had any issues with them becoming to short. You have to buy these. I will be buying more for sure.

On first try-on I LOVE these jeans! So much! They’re the first jeans that really fit me since pregnancy. But shortly after wearing them, the flaws really started to show: 1- the belly cover is nude/skin color (why??) it makes it so you absolutely have to have a long shirt to cover it, or it looks really weird 2- the jean area barely comes up enough to cover your crotch area. I don’t know why they didn’t bring the jeans line up higher on my stomach, it would have made it easier to wear with normal shirts. And when I sit down, it says low on my bottom too. Not super flattering. 3- they don’t stay up very well. With the stretchy fabric, and the jeans not coming up very high, they just sag a bit. This is definitely not a deal breaker, just a side note. Overall these jeans fit SO good on my legs, and made me feel happy to wear jeans again. I’m currently 140 lbs (120 pre pregnancy) and I’m 6 months pregnant. If you have lots of long shirts, it’s definitely worth it to have nice jeans! I probably would have gone with the dark wash next time, as the stretchy material matches the jean color better. I’m sad that the medium color has a nude stretchy belly, but maybe they can change that in the future.

On the bright side, They fit perfectly! I love that they take into consideration people like me who buy some pants only to have them dragging across the floor because we’re short lol. I’m 5’0 and 90lbs and got the xs short for anyone needing a reference. Upon opening the package however, it has a weird smell. I’m honestly not sure how to describe it. It’s not an icky smell like fish or sweat, just chemical-ly and weird. Hopefully it’ll go away after I wash it. Also, important to note that it does needs to be steamed to get rid of wrinkles! Other than that, I’m really happy with these pants😊
Once you put the pants on they fit perfect until you start moving around and they stretch. The pants feel nice! Am 5’5” 195lb and ordered a size 13

One of the appeals to this style is the wide legs. For women like me, with rather thick thighs, these pants are liberating and offer much comfort. These did not disappoint in that aspect. But overall, the length was a huge drawback (reduce a star). They were like 5 inches too long. I attempted to roll the waist band to reduce the length but they were still too long and doing so caused the pants to bunch in the rear end. I attempted to roll them from the bottom at no avail; the cuff would not stay. Nevertheless, the feel was super soft with rather shallow pockets for such a relaxed style (reduce a star).I did order my normal size 12. For reference, I am 5'6" at 175lbs with a waist of 35". I felt like the elastic would help to frame my waist, but I wish I had ordered a smaller size because I did not feel any curve was sightly in these pants.

One of the influencers that I follow on IG keeps raving about these pants, so I bit the bullet and bought these in both the black and the brown color ways. I'm so glad that I did! These pants are comfortable. The material is lightweight which is great for HI weather. They seem versatile, you can dress these up or down and best of all its short girl friendly! They are high-waisted and usually that doesn't work out too well with my short torso but these pants I don't mind. For reference I'm 5'0 130-140 lbs and I ordered a size M in both colors.

One thing I would say is Amazon recommended I get an eight and I followed the recommendations because they had my measurements correct at 5‘8“ and 145 pounds. But the eight Was Way too large. I’m going to exchange the eight for the 66 fits perfectly, they’re super comfortable and feel very good quality for the Price point. I ordered the black and a six and they’re great the one complaint other than size I had about the blue pair I ordered and an eight was they smell kind of toxic I don’t know if it’s the dye or if it’ll wash away But it has a very strong odor compared to the black pair I got. Hopefully the pair I exchange it for will not have that same intense smellu

Only downside for me, is have a steamer handy because this fabric wrinkles easily. It feels kind of silky. Lot of perks though- it’s very breezy and comfortable and makes for a cute outfit! For reference, I’m 5’6” and bought a size large which fits comfortably (I normally buy a large in pants). With the waistband resting at my belly button, the pant legs land at my feet, but I wear wedges which help lift them a bit. Each pant leg is about the size of my body width and the waistband is very stretchy. If you are shorter than 5’6 you might need to get the pant legs hemmed.
Only giving 4 stars because the short length is almost too short. and that’s coming from someone who is 5’2! Other than that, I love them! I’m a size 8, 175 lbs, and ordered a large.

Oooh these jeans are so fun. I love the fit and feel. The jean material has a decent stretch and did not come with any funky odors. I did wash in medium heat and machine dried. These jeans fit perfectly in length and size in an x-small. I didn't have to roll the bottoms as they fall right to my ankles, but felt like it while wearing my huaraches. For reference, I am 5ft 2 in, 120-125 lbs, athletic build, and size small/x-small fits me best. So, do order to size. All the stitching and sewing is done well with no open seams, overlapping of material or loose hanging thread. I do recommend these jeans. I'm really loving the cut.

Order the size 2 regular. For reference my measurements are 24.5" waist and 35" hip. The jeans fit perfectly around my waist without needing a belt which is a rare occurrence for me. Besides the sizing, however, the jeans are way over priced and the color is off. As you can see, I ordered the "regular blue" yet, the actual color that they came as is not as blue as pictured and actually seems like what the "light blue" option looks like. The material is also not real denim, so there is no vintage look to them at all. The material is like a jegging: super stretchy. Also, the button is really flimsy and seems like it will fall off easily. I returned them because $46 dollars for these is over priced.

Order up a size or two, I ordered an up one size to an XL and they’re still a little snug. Luckily they have a good stretch. I like them and will probably but another paid but go up to an XXL. For reference I am 5’7, 185 lbs and curvy. Length on these pants are perfect

Order way up. I ordered a 1x/2x, here's why. I read many of the reviews carefully and referenced their size chart. I was reading... too small, annoying stripes, etc. That's because these are small. They are super stretchy, but have a snug waistband. They are surprisingly slimming... if you order right. I am 5'5" and 150 and a 3-c-section belly. I usually wear size medium or size 8-10. This is why I ordered the 1\2x and they fit absolutely comfy and amazing. I am not the type that orders small and tries to stretch to the max. I'd rather order big and be extremely comfortable. These have no negatives on any part of my body for ordering big and no overly stretchy lines. I just think their size chart and measurements are like Japanese and European that run extremely small and make us feel fat. Lol. I hope this helps. I will absolutely order more colors and in the bigger size.

Order your true size, don’t size down. Man, I wish I hadn’t listened to the reviews saying to size down because they thought these ran big. I’m 5’6”, 140#, and normally a size medium (28 in Levi’s) bottoms for reference. I ordered these shortalls in a size small so that they wouldn’t be too big, per the suggestion of other buyers. But unfortunately, they are more fitted than I prefer. I would have been much happier with these in my regular size. They are still wearable and fit like a true small, so I can’t fault the size chart or the garment. These just aren’t going to be as comfy casual on me as they would have been in a medium. I’m still living out my 90s glory days in these regardless. Just trust in Levi’s size chart and order your true size to get the proper vintage fit.
Orderd a medium im 5.4" and weigh140lb. Its not a light weight fabric its more of a jean material and i love it well made.
Ordered 26x36 because I'm 6' tall. The 36" inseam is actually a 34". Pretty disappointed.
Ordered a Large and they fit perfect. For reference: I’m 5’5 150lbs size 8 in jeans. Super cute, comfy, and light weight!

Ordered a medium and small in Apricot just to compare sizes. I am 5’2 about 135 pounds. The small fit, but was a little snug especially with the elastic and they didn’t sit slouchy at the bottom like I expected. The mediums are perfect in length, stretch, and size. Super cozy and not suffocating at all. Neither were severely see through either. Smalls are the first photo, medium is second. I am keeping the mediums — exactly as expected!
Ordered a medium, and it fit great. I’m 5 ft 1 with smaller waist and bigger thighs and this skirt fit like a glove.

Ordered a size 16 short. I’m 5’0” and usually wear a size 16. This are just long enough for me to wear heels with. I love the slightly oversize fit without it being too baggy. Very comfortable and great quality

Ordered a size small and absolutely love this skort!! I am 145 lbs and wear a size 4/6. Super stretchy and comfortable. Ordered a size small in tan and size medium in black for different styles but both fit super cute! I’m 5’5 and size 27-28 in jeans.

Ordered a size small, am 5'7" 148lbs, 36b, 37"-30"-40". Fits well & comfortably. Very high quality satin, slippery and was very comfortable all night and well into the next day. Entire set is flowy and loose, as you may know satin is not particularly forgiving, but I am satisfied with the size I got. However, it is a bit tight innthe chest-I prefer my clothes fitted usually so and it is in no way restrictive or uncomfortable-that being said, if you're at all bigger than me in the chest, I do recommend you seek a size above. The color is gorgeous, and the whole set is just very high quality and finely made. Sleek and elegant
Ordered a size small. Next day delivery. 5’9, 125 pounds. Super comfy and well worth the value and will be ordering in other colors.
Ordered a small in white since I already had the dark denim. Fits great. I’m 5’1” and 100 lbs. Should have ordered this brand in the first place in white. Wrangler just didn’t fit well.

Ordered a small in white. I’m 5’3 and 120lbs. Being so short a wide leg makes me look shapeless and frumpy so I like that these are tapered but it isn’t too tight and still gives a nice relaxed look. Fabric isn’t stiff & length is right. The only thing I’d change is the length of the drawstring. It’s too long so I’ll probably trim. I’ll also probably buy one more color. Would like to pair with a wht linen button up opened w/ crop top or bikini underneath all summer and to Hawaii.

Ordered an xl and they are way big by at least 2 sizes. I am 5'8" 178lbs also the bottom of pant legs are wide. The picture in the post showed a snug fit and this is not the case even if they would have fit the leg is a wide flare which also doesn't work. Have to return.

Ordered both a Large ( my recommended size per Amazon) and an XL per reviewers saying it was tight in the chest (I’m a 38B). The XL came first and fit fine with a shirt underneath and comfortable. Then the Large came and it also fit comfortably so I decided to measure them and found they were identical in size 🤷🏼‍♀️. Both were 21” across at chest (42”) and 33 ¾” in length from top of shoulder. I’m 5’4 1/2” and it is above the knee so a great length for me. Both are great quality denim fabric, well made and flattering on as well as comfortable. Both tags say 85% cotton 15% polyester ( not spandex). As to stretch, there is stretch up and down but Not side to side as one might expect. I decided to keep both as it’s such a great versatile wardrobe piece that is hard to find. Just don’t know if this is a fluke that both sizes were identical or what. 🤷🏼‍♀️

Ordered dark grey and black. Tag says not to put in dryer so I hung them up outside. Both were purchased as Long so I could wear with heels and both were long prior to washing. Grey pants SHRANK 8 INCHES! I get that it's hot here in AZ right now but good lord this is ridiculous. The black pants only shrunk 5ish so they're still OK for regular shoes but these were supposed to fit someone 5'8-6ft! I'm 5'2 and the grey ones don't even touch the floor now! Don't buy if you're actually tall and live in a hot climate I guess. 🙁

Ordered medium petite and they fit perfect. I'm 5'3" and weigh 135 for reference. Ordered them in black and brown. The black pair seem to be a smidge shorter. I haven't measured them to see if they actually are. I'm able to wear them with sneakers, suede slippers or clogs and length is perfect. If I went with regular length they would drag the floor.They can be dressed up to be work appropriate or worn for casual or workout wear.I'm probably going to order in grey and cameo (if they come back in stock).

Ordered short, received mismarked 31in inseam. Jeans are adorable and fit great except for length. I am 5'1, 120, about 36.5 hip, 27 waist, muscular legs. So they sent wrong size and they must have issues with production line. But flippin cute so 2 stars and I might try again...

Ordered size XL. Fits as expected. They are stretchy. I'm on a weight loss journey and normally would wear a XXL. I'm down 30 pounds. I'm 5'7" and currently weigh 197. The waist hits at my bellybutton and bottom of the legs hit below my ankles, not touching the floor. Satisfied with overall fit, length, and quality.
Ordered size XXS in two styles but only keep one. The all buttons style seem to be more flatter. Size XXS fits me well for 5ft2 107lbs. Furry inside, stretchy. A little pricey but worth it.

Ordered the aged blue and expected it to look like as shown in the product image(s). The color of the shirt received has a much faded look. The fabric is thin and light. A bit disappointed with the extra fade above and below the pockets. It came all wrinkled, which should be expected for 100% cotton material. Not a big deal as we always wash and iron clothing before wearing them. Ordered size small for a 5’4” tall person. The shirt has a relaxed fit, and will be a good shirt to wear during summer days. Looks good on dark shorts / pants. If not for the excess faded look, I would have given it 5 stars.
Ordered the khaki and dusty lavender in medium. The khaki were longer and slightly larger overall. The other color fit perfect. Will return khaki and order in small. I'm 5'2, 125lbs.

Ordered the medium, but returned for the large because the other size was tight in shoulders and a bit short even for me. This straight out of the package, so I have no idea how it washes up. I am 5 feet 4 inches and weigh 155 lbs for reference. I like the color and the weight of fabric is as expected for denim.
Ordered these for a 80’s party. They were a perfect fit. I’m 5’8 weigh 140 and ordered size Small

Ordered these for part of my Lollipop Guild Costume for a Wizard of Oz Movie Themed Event. They are SO soft and stretchy. I was extremely comfortable in them all night. No pinching, pulling, tugging. Just straight up comfort. I'm 5'4 and about 210 lbs with a butt. Hips are 50in, I probably could have gotten away with an XL but I'm wearing a XXL in this photo.

Ordered these in the coffee color, size 4 for my daughter - she's 5'5" & 112lbs. The material feels nice, soft with a little stretch, and the thickness is just right - not too thin & not too thick. The construction is well done with neat seams, sturdy zipper, and easy buttoning. As far as the fit goes, these were perfect before washing & drying. The length was just right. After washing & drying we both thought they shrank too much lengthwise, and will look weird if she wears these with any type of heeled shoe. So, if you're on the tall side, don't put these in the dryer! Also, the color is solid brown, not the acid washed look in the pics on the product page. Four stars because they're too short & color is misleading.

Ordered this dress for a concert and super happy with the way it shaped me body. It’s stretchy and firm. Also very comfy. I would recommend this dress. I’m 5ft and weight 140 and got a size small and it fits great.

Ordered this for a Red Clay Strays concert. Was pleasantly surprised how flattering it is. the material has bit of stretch to it. only reason i didnt rate it a 5 stars because the material and buttons arent the best of quality. but who cares for a cute fun faux denim dress like this! i am 5’7 and 140 lbs with an athletic build.

Ordered two pair. One was legit, the other knock-off. The 4 stars is for the legit pair. 36-28-40 - Ordered Size 9. A little snug, but they're stretchy so they work and look great on. I'm a fan of Vibrant (so long as you're receiving the real deal).

Originally bought these bcuz someone left a review about how comfortable these were and versatile they can be dressed down for a more casual brunch or dressed up for a corporate office or event setting. They weren’t lying , these are so cozy I have even used them as sweatpants. I bought a small and it fits perfect. I am just under 5’4 and they are perfect length when worn with doc martens but without the slight heel they would drag. So if you’re a short girly you may want to wear with heels or shoes with a thicker platform. The material is soft and sturdy. Overall this brand is great
Originally got a medium and was too big. Returned and bought a small that fits perfectly. I’m 5’4” and about 125lbs.

Originally I had purchased the large, as I am 5’8”, size 10, 34DD. It was too large, so I went down a size and the medium fits great! I was concerned the buttons would pucker across my chest, but there is still ample room. My one complaint is that it smells horrible, but that is easily fixable!

Originally order my normal size large but the waist was way too big. I returned and got a medium, it fit perfectly! Its actually a stretchy type of thick material not denim. Will definitely be ordering in more colors!! 160lbs 5’7 for reference.

Other reviews stated they were true to size but I'm 5'4" 130lbs so I ordered a size 6 and these are skin tight on me. Too tight for a business conference and they pucker at the pocket so I had to send back.

Over the years, I've gone from living stretchy jeans, to essentially trying to LIVE in super stretchy, jegging type jeans. I don't have a lot but I want more. I'd love to look good while feeling extremely comfy, so I took a chance on these. I am 5'3 and have a 32" inseam but these are almost perfect as they are. They have a cute fit. I'm not crazy about the little stitches on the back pockets (seems unnecessary) but the fit is just cute and flattering! Style wise, I love that these look like regular jeans as they have real zipper and button closure, real pockets, but have the comfort of Jennings or pajama denim. I want these in a million colors!

Overall I really like these pants! I’m 5’ 9”, 195 lbs and ordered a Large in the light apricot color. It’s definitely a light creamy taupe color, not apricot. If you want them to be very oversized I would say size up but I like how the large looks on me. I’m also 15 weeks pregnant and the waistband has a ton of stretch so I will probably be able to wear them even when my belly is bigger. The fabric is nice and light for the summer but a little bit scratchy when I’m sitting down. I will be keeping them!

Overall I'm pleasantly surprised. They are a sturdy/thick material and seem to be well made. The length is a bit too high-watery for me but that's par for the course being 5'7". Some day I'll find pants thar crumple up a little on top of my kicks like all my short(er) friends... but until that day comes: the option to tie the bottom of the pant leg is my saving grace. The pants sit pretty much waste high, which has been my overwhelming preference from about age 33 onward. Overall a decent purchase. Oh and they're very comfortable too.

Overall pants fit great besides the crotch area was weirdly made I’m 5’5 165

Overall, awesome pants! Pros: - Long enough! Probably even too long (I got large tall) but as a tall girl who rarely finds long enough inseams, I have a tendency to overdo it just to relish in the rare experience of too much length. - super comfy. The outside is smooth fabric and the inside is a thin, soft fleece. Warm enough for cool weather but not so insulated that they're a deep-winter-only pair. - pockets are deep enough, ankles are snug enough to look good without being tight Cons: - the sizing chart was a bit wonky, so out of caution I sized up to a large knowing I'd rather be comfy than sausage-cased. There's a lot of extra room, so I really should have stuck to the usual medium. For reference, I'm 5'10", 160lbs, and typically wear size 8 pants. I have a 33" inseam. - the outside is kinda shiny. Coupled with the extra material, they feel a little like 80's windbreaker pants without the awful swishing sounds The photos are done after washing and drying to see if there was any shrinkage (anyone how has felt the disappointment of bringing home pants that were long enough in the store to end up having yet another pair of slightly too short house pants after the first wash knows why I waited to review) and I didn't detect any shrinking, so that's fantastic for buying true to size. These pants have me excited for autumn weather so I can get cozy in these pants that'll actually keep my ankles warm!

Overall, I like the skirt a lot it came out perfect. Although, I will say size down. I’m 5’1 and 198lbs, I got an XL and it was pretty big/long on me. I had to cut it to get the right length and bring the waist in a little. The red color is a tad brighter than I expected and it was fine. Tip: get it steamed or iron really good to get those fold lines out from it being inside the package, makes it look 10x better. Would buy again

Overall, these are nice wide leg sweatpants. They are cute and comfortable. In terms of size, I would say that they are true to size and they also have a lot of stretch to them. On me, they're tight on the upper legs like yoga pants, and wide leg below the knee. They're also long enough on me, which is nice because I'm tall (5'11"). Only taking off one star because it says they are dressy pants, but they certainly are not. They are like sweatpants. So describing them as lounge pants would certainly be more accurate. I was hoping to wear these out, but I would only wear them around the house.

I bought black and blue and they fit well but can be uncomfortable during that time of the month or when dealing with bloating, which may also depend on the size you order. I’m 5’7”, 145-150 lbs with an hour glass shape and bought the (M) size. I could’ve gotten the large but that’s okay. They are form fitting and comfortable outside of pms.

Perfection! I bought this in both White and Wishful Blue. They are both a soft, comfy denim fabric with a slightly fitted shape. Not too fitted, but not that typical stiff boxy jeans jacket. At 5’2” somewhere between a 12 -14, I was pleased to find the size Large was the perfect fit. The “hem” hits me right above/at the hips, which is perfect for wearing over a spring/summer dress. I love the pockets, which are big enough to actually tuck your hands into. I like that it’s not too distressed. No rips or holes; just the frayed hem and a few “worn” spots. The wishful blue color is so pretty, It’s a nice worn blue. Not white washed. Not acid washed. And definitely not that weird yellowish dirty denim that I hate. Another reviewer called it cropped, which is not my opinion at all. It doesn’t cover my tush, but it hits lower than my waist. Delivery was quick. It arrived in one day. Hope this helps you find your best size and color.

Photo size reference: I am 5’6” and 130 lbs. I normally wear a size 4/27 but decided to order the 28 in the trouser jeans. For my desk job, I was glad for the extra inch as there is very little stretch in the fabric. However, the denim did lose some shape after sitting a few hours and I did not love the “fupa” effect of the loosened lower abdomen area. Super high-waisted, with hip and back pockets. My silhouette was fly when I first put the jeans on but by the end of the day I had some lumps and bumps from all angles. I absolutely counted down the minutes until I could change though because the jeans are not comfortable for cross-leg sitters. The stiff fabric dug into all my crevices and made a few more. My opinion is that Joes Jeans is a sought after brand of high-quality denim…but this particular pair is best worn standing for this wide hipped female.

Pictured: Front and back of the pants before and after wash. To me, the term "Palazzo pants" evokes a longer, more flowy silhouette. The length feels more akin to long gauchos or very baggy capris. The inseam on these pants are 24" so bear that in mind when you purchase. They must have used models that are maybe 5' 5" or 5' 6" for the hem to almost hit the ground. Or maybe the photos are edited. I got these pants thinking they'd at least hit my ankles or skim the top of my feet. No such luck. The cotton fabric feels like light denim or twill. It's kinda stiff, so the pantlegs retain their voluminous shape. The contrasting white stitching on the seams give these pants a very casual look. I'm not 100% sold on the wide white string tie. I may remove it altogether since the waistband is elasticized. Be sure to air dry as these pants are cotton and will shrink up even higher!

Probably closer to a 4.5 really. I went with the Fit Type: 28" Inseam, Size: X-Large in Color: Khaki. For reference I'm over 50 years old. Height 5'6", weight 170lbs measurements Bust: 43 Waist: 34 Hips: 43. The fabric on these pants reminds me of the polyester pants my mother would wear to work in. It's rather a bit of a heavier weight with no stretch other than the back elastic band. The feel is smooth and it's so loose that it doesn't really come in contact with the skin much at all. When it does, like when sitting, it hasn't aggravated my sensitive skin. It has been through the washer and dryer, than sat over night in the dryer before I folded them up and stored in a basket for a few days before I got to this review. Yes, they are a bit wrinkled but not as badly as I would have expected given the way I treated them. They do iron out well. Otherwise, there was no harm in the cleaning cycle, didn't bleed, shrink or twist, pill or pick up excess lint. Really what drew me to these pants was that they were different. Yes, they are a bit large on me, but I like to order a size larger so I can alter if needed. Only minor alterations will be needed to make these a really nice set of slacks for me. That's the best compliment, in my opinion, if it's worth messing with to make them exactly what you want and these feel like they are. Overall, I'm happy with them but can't see wearing them in warmer weather. Peace Peace

It has an elastic waistband that will give you muffin tops. Finally, the rise is just over 8.5" which is too low. 9-9.5 would be ideal, and would probably solve the muffin top issue. Overall they are cute so I will keep them. I just wish they came slightly higher and didn't have an elastic waistband and I would seriously buy every color.

Purchased based on the photos, the green tie dye print (it isn’t actual tie dye) was not matched up right. So there was a litter colored blob above the belly. Real issues are with the fabric. Stretch fabric on the 2XL was clingy not flowy like the photos. It was almost like it had static but it didn’t. The sizing was off. 2XL was almost smaller than the XL. Both fit way too close to the body. I am a size 14 and these fit like a size Large. The inseam is very small if you wear these pants as they should be- with the gathered elastic up high. If I pulled them lower to get the length right, there was sagging in the crotch and in the back. Not flattering. Seller should get better fabric, and make sure of consistency with sizes and fabrics. I am almost 5’9” but these don’t fit well for me when worn as intended. NOT recommended for tall women.

Relevant measurements: 5'2", about 125-130 lb, 26" to 28" waist, 34" hips I usually don't buy jeans for myself, since I find I have a hard time finding jeans that fit me in both the waist and hips -- there's an 8-10 inch difference between them! Nevertheless, I decided that I wanted to splurge and try on some name-brand jeans for once in my life. I ordered the size 27W/32L in Medium Wash. I don't like these jeans. I'll start off with the positives. They are well-constructed, and I doubt they'll fall apart anytime soon. They are definitely the most high-waisted jeans I've ever purchased for myself, which is what I was looking for. And, surprisingly enough, there wasn't a ton of excess fabric bunching up by my ankles -- a problem I commonly have as a 5'2" woman. The color of the jeans is great, and they have an almost "rustic" feel to them, in my opinion. The price is what you'd expect for something by AA. But the negatives, oh boy. The most prominent one: these jeans are THICK and NOT STRETCHY at all. It was a pain trying to get my feet and ankles through the cuffs -- and an even bigger pain trying to get them out. These aren't jeans you can just "slip on" in the morning -- it'll take you a minute to get them on. Then, of course, the thickness -- it means quality, but it also means that I feel constricted when walking and sitting down, to the point where it's actually uncomfortable. Then, even though my waist is a 26", there were still 2-3 inches of gapping between my waist and the jeans. Conclusion: I'm returning these jeans.
Returned because the length was too long for me (5’2”, 165, Size L) and the barrel cut sat to long on me. Lots of stretch… and those pockets…just would not stay in!

Returned the bright blue day color, that cost 3 times as much but kept the elevated retro color that was a steal at $16.00 and took a chance on boiling them for 30 minutes on stove top and drying on sanitize cycle for maximum shrinkage. See photo laying on top of retuned pair, the length shrunk almost two inches. Still need to wear with heals, so not for everyday at almost 70 years old, but they make me feel young again. I am 5’3” tall with longer legs and shorter torso. I bought the size 12 short which did shrink all over so if you are big from the knees up these may not be for you. They were scary long looking out of the package but 99% cotton does shrink nicely. If I manage to shrink them a little more they could rate 6 stars!

Amazing for fall *chefs kiss* looks just like the picture. Pants arrived kinda stiff but expected due to the great quality of Levi’s and relaxed after the first wear. Saw these tagged by an influencer, so I wanted to try them out! They are super cute, and exactly what I was looking for. The material is soft, and they are the right amount of oversized. I would size up, typically I consider myself a small in bottoms but I ordered a medium in these. I am 5’8 and 125lbs and the medium fits perfectly. They are cute to scrunch a little with crew socks or just wearing plain with a bodysuit or cropped tank. Highly recommend!

Searched Amazon in-depth to find the perfect warm cargo sweats with pockets and found these. I’m 5’8 and 130 pounds, I ordered a medium and it’s a tad big on me but the waist is perfect. I like the oversized look and they will shrink in the dryer. Thought about ordering a small but figured it might not be long enough for a tall girl. Ended up ordering 3 colors! Black, blue, and burgundy! Perfect for the fall and winter months!

Second baby so bump is showing a little early. I’m 13 weeks and a few days. Normally 130ish pounds and I think 134 now. I think I’m 5’1” so they are a little long but I just roll them. I can see myself being able to wear these basically until my third tri. I ordered size small

See through white trousers with almost no stretch and not flattering for thick thighs. I don’t know why I thought these would look good on me lol, lesson learned. seem like they would be cute but i’m 5’3 and this length doesn’t work for my fit lol

Seriously these are fantastic. They are so comfortable and flattering I'm gonna buy another pair now❤️❤️❤️❤️ I'm 5'8 and 225lbs

Seriously….I have never bought something so fast! These are literally perfect! Wide leg bottom, plenty of stretch…perfect cuff bottom! set was cute and comfy, fit true to size! i also like the ruching on the shirt- super flattering. the quality’s not great but you get what you pay for. i’ve had a couple stitches pop after wearing twice and the pants shrunk a couple inches after washing as recommended which was disappointing since i’m 5’8 and usually can’t find pants long enough. overall would recommend!

Shopping in this pandemic without being able to try on is a nightmare. Shopping online has its moments and especially difficult buying jeans without trying them on. Played sports growing up but out of shape now, I’m 37 and 160lbs near 5’9 height. Shown in dark wash size large- I'm 5'8, 175lbs, 42-32-41, usually wear a size 8/10 in pants. These jeans still have a strong yuck smell after first wash. I imagine that it will dissipate over a few more washings. Note that the "dark blue" wash is much darker in person than it seemed online.

Side seam wraps around the front and looks cheap. See photos. A bit see through in the natural oatmeal color (see pic). Fits well. I got a large and my measurements are 31 waist, 42.5 hip, 25 thigh. It could be looser in the thigh but still fits and I can sit down fine.

Sidefeel Women Casual Loose Mid Rise Cargo Jeans GREAT I can see my belly button. I can breathe. Not high-waisted. They sit more-so on my hips. While I don’t really want to go back to all 90s styles, I enjoy getting away from crazy tight/too high-waisted pants when I can! POCKETS The front pockets fit my iPhone SE. It’s a little awkward when I first sit down (the phone digs into my hip a little), but it’s not like the phone is going to fall out. I think I will eventually not notice it. The back pockets also fit my iPhone SE, but when I sit down it sticks out quite a bit and I am not sure my phone will stay secure. OKAY The material is heavier than I expected, but it is not uncomfortable. Without shoes on these hit the top of my feet/down to the back of my heel. They almost drag on the floor – almost but not quite. So, the length is okay for me. I am 5’8’’. I don’t know if it will be an issue for others. SIZING If I followed their size chart based on my measurements only, I could have potentially gone with a size 4 which lists “hip 37.4”. However, I chose to follow the suggestions of previous reviewers to size up to a size 6 which lists “hip 38.39”. I am happy with my size choice. For reference, I am 5'8" and 125 lbs, with an athletic build, and my current measurements are bust 35.5, 28.5 inches for my waist, and 37.5 inches for my hips. OVERALL There is nothing necessarily wrong with the style of these pants, but there is nothing spectacular either. They are definitely for casual wear – not crazy flattering or unflattering for that matter. So, I don’t not recommend them. They are high quality and a break from the norm – you’ll have to decide for yourself on the style.

Silky smooth and shiny. So comfortable I have even slept in them. I am 6'-2" and 185 lbs and the length and waist were perfect. I would need larger curvy hips to fill out these pants correctly. I have guy hips.

Since I was reading about the discrepancy in the fit and concern of shrinking, I ordered two pair of capri-green, a medium and a large. As you can see, they're different color and different fabric, which just shows the inconsistency of this product. They feel different, as they're different fabric, but both are nice. The length of both are right above the ankle. I'm 5'4" 145 lbs. The medium fits me better, but the elastic waistband is a little tighter than I'd like it to be, and I'm worried about the shrinking as it's the 100% cotton one. The large on fits fine too though. Neither one says to dry clean. I'm keeping both.
Since I’m 5’1 I frayed the ends even more and now they’re the perfect length.

Since the reviews on this jacket was the deciding factor to purchase, I new I had to post a review as well. I'm 5'8" tall and 125 lbs. I have long arms and torso with an athletic build. size 12 in pants 5’7 ~160lbs overall, I love these pants. I love the color the style it’s very comfortable since it’s loose. Would definitely buy again.

Size 22 Plus - regular length - Black 5'2" - 235lbs - short torso - large booty I've got what I call a " booty shelf". My bum sticks out a lot, and you could almost set stuff on it. So to be fair, a lot of women my height and weight may not have this issue... The waist gap is big with these jeans. No way can I wear them. When they slide down, which they will inevitably do, they'll still show way too much booty/undies, especially if I bend over and/or my shirt rides up. The regular length is long on me, but that's not unusual. Otherwise the fit seems fine in the hips/thighs. I'm returning them, so of course I don't know how much they'll shrink in the wash. These will probably work great for people without a booty shelf. Since I can't wear them I'm giving 4 stars, but for a different shaped body they could easily be 5 star jeans.

Size Large has a 28-36" elastic waist, 42" hips, 28" inseam, and 12" high rise. The elastic is medium-light, not too firm, feels pretty comfy even at 36". They are definitely wide leg and are a bit excessively baggy on me but at least I can move around easily in them. It's not entirely clear what these pants are made of but the outer layer is a lovely nice quality sweater material that seems likely to be a blend of synthetic and natural materials, and the inner layer is a synthetic microfleece. The color '01 Nude' is pretty similar to the listing photo, a bit darker, they just photograph more yellow. The pockets are pretty roomy. They do show pantylines despite the thickness. The pants are a bit heavy but it has belt loops if you need a belt to keep them up. It's too early in the year to test them but they seem plenty warm to me and will probably be okay down to like a good 10F is what I would guess. They look quite sharp and fashionable but feel like cozy sweatpants, I like them. I would recommend to wash on cold and hang dry, no dryer ever.

Size M and I am 5'5" 130 lbs. They are cute, much thicker than I expected. Wasn't sure how to rate sheer! They are not sheer, at least not the rust color. They might be long for someone shorter than me. They are very high waisted as well.
Size medium fit nice and would buy again 140lbs and 5’2

Size Medium. I’m 5”7 & 165ish lbs. The straps are adjustable to help with the low cut front. I wear a 36C and size 12 pants. This is on the shorter side for the tall girlies so be mindful. Other than that I got sooo many compliments on this.

Size reference: I am 5’6” and 124-128 lbs. I normally wear a size 4 in jeans and dress pants. After reading other reviews, I ordered the 4 SHORT in gray from iChosy. The fit is perfect with the legs being exactly right for flats. I LOVE these pants. They are soft and super stretchy, like sweat pants disguised as dress pants. The fit is flattering to my waistline and has enough give that I am comfortable when seated at my desk. The fabric does not wrinkle during normal well making these pants a contender for meeting days and travel. The fly is button and zipper. The cotton blend material is thicker than other slacks fabric so I’ve also been warmer at work. I am writing this review as I wear these pants in my office for the first time. I have received compliments from everyone I’ve seen and I bragged to my other half how beyond comfortable they are. In fact, I already ordered the black pair.

Size reference: I am 5’6” and 126 lbs, wearing the size small recommended on the size chart. I normally wear a size 4 in dress pants and jeans and have a 27” waist and 37” rear. Fit is true-to size, and there is plenty of stretch in the waist if you are between sizes. I absolutely love these pants. I wore them the first chance I had to my business casual desk job. The material is gauzy, soft, and slightly stretchy. The waist is wide-band elastic with a draw string. The legs are really wide and the waist is high-high (like bottom of rib cage). They came without shipping wrinkles and did fine after all day wear. The legs are flowy and the high waist is flattering with a slim fit top. I wear tighter pants, skinny and slim mostly, for work most days so these wide leg palazzo pants are a nice change. I feel so comfortable while seated and stylish when I’m greeting people. Grace Karin delivered again and I’m certain I will be wearing this pair regularly. I can wear with accessories to work or could throw on a crop tank and sandals to wear casually on vacation. Either option is cute and comfy, 2 of my favorite things! Absolutely no complaints, 5/5 stars

Size reference: I am 5’6” and 126 lbs. Almost all of my jeans are US size 4 or 27 and the majority of my elastic waist pants are medium (leggings, jeggings, joggers, sweats, etc.). The size chart suggested a small for sizes 4 and 6 but I decided to order my stretchy pant size of medium. Because of the cotton and rayon fabric, I decided to prepare for a little shrinkage. That was a mistake. Material: Thin, buttery soft, with just a hint of stretch. Lovely shade of army green with an elastic waist, functioning front hip pockets, and drawstring. Opinion: Definitely go by the size chart and downsize if you are on the line. These pants are so large on me that I can’t even cinch them with the drawstring to stay on. Way too big in the waist and baggy in the rear and legs. Honestly, the small probably would be extra roomy as well because these are not even close to my other mediums. They seem well made and I love the softness of the material. Definitely not a good choice for snow days but great for warmer weather. If they had fit, I would have used them for business casual attire at the office during the summer.

Size reference: I am 5’6” and 127 lbs. I normally wear a size 4 in jeans and can wear either small or medium in stretchy pants. Based off prior experience, I ordered small from Amazon Essentials because they tend run big. The fit of the small is still a little big, similar to what a medium in brick and mortar store brands would run. The smooth elastic waist has a little gap when I’m standing. I have shorter legs and the leg openings touch the top of my feet. I actually like the looser fit because if they were skin tight I think they would look more like sweats than slacks. Description: I really like how these feel. The stretch of yoga pants but made with better material so I can pass these as business casual with the right top. The tag will have to be cut out because the corner does scratch my lower back. Also, and this is an issue I have had from AMZ brands before, the black looks slightly faded compared to other dress pants. It makes it hard to pair with other black pieces from my closet because the difference is quite noticeable. Other than that, I like these comfy work pants. Deducted a star for color and scratchy tag. (Didn’t deduct a star for fit because the size chart does show small to be about 2 inches larger in the waist than standard small).

Size reference: I am 5’6” and 127 lbs. I normally wear a size 4 in pants and jeans. I ordered these pants in size small. The fit is true to size in the waist. The back is elastic so there is a little give if you are between sizes. When standing, the pants are full length but when I sit the cuffs come up to mid-shin. Description: I love the design with the pleating. I can get by with pairing with heels to wear to work because the ankles don’t have the bunched elastic jogger look. I also like the real button, zipper fly, and belt loops for the same reason. The outside material is soft, like yoga pants. The inside is lined with slick polyester. There is some stretch but not like true sweat pants. I do worry that the pants will snag easily because of the material. Also, I hope the rayon doesn’t shrink if I accidentally dry these. Recommendation: I love these pants. They are comfy like loungewear but dressed up enough for business casual. I ordered the green and there is some heathering in the background. If not for that, I would probably go ahead and order the black but heathered black looks too faded to pass as work pants. This is the 8th pair of pants I’ve gotten lately and by far the most comfy! I highly recommend!!!!

Size reference: I am 5'6" and 129 lbs, normally a size 4 in pants/jeans. The size chart suggested a small but I wanted a loose waist so I went with a medium. I am pleased with the up-size! The legs are full length, the waist is not digging into me but doesn't look too big. Description: I have ordered many wide leg pants so I expected the fabric to be chiffon type and flowy. However, the material is firm polyester, like a blazer with some stiffness and shape. Not thin and not see through. I love the pleats on the waistline as it does a lot flatter the under-navel region. They stick out a bit which helps camouflage pooches and sharp hip bones. The hip pockets are big enough to be functional. The waistline is extra-high, which I like from the front. There is something weird with the back view, perhaps the faux pockets are in the wrong place proportionally. As for quality of construction, the pants are well made but not name brand dupe worthy. I initially ordered strictly as work pants. However, after trying them on I think they would be cute with a crop tank and new sneakers for the weekend as well. Recommendation: I love them. I like the way the feel, look, and fit. I've already added them to my watch list and will order the navy if they go on sale. The pale pink is perfect for the spring. At the current price of $29.99, these pants exceeded expectations.

Size reference: I’m 5’6” and 128 lbs Witt a 27.5” waist and 37” hips. I wear size 4/27 in pants/jeans and have both small and medium in stretchy pants. The size chart showed the small from Noahella to have a 28.34” waist and 41.34 inch hips. I think the small is just a little snug on my waist so the button looks slightly pulled. The length is perfect for flats. Description: The material feels and looks like “real” slacks, opposed to the cheap polyester look that snags easily. The fabric is 100% polyester but looks like chiffon and is flowy. The pleated front gives a dressier style and the wide legs are comfortable but look sophisticated. The back waistband is elastic but the front is flat with a button/zipper fly. There are belt loops, deep functional hip pockets, and faux back pockets. Recommendation: I love these pants for work! I feel stylish and comfortable when I wear them. The high waist is flattering with a fitted top or tucked-in loose top. I would be okay with wearing these for business travel, meetings, church, and wedding/baby showers.

Size small 5’5”, 146-150, 32DDD, These feel like loose fit pajama pants, so if you want a heavyweight fabric skip them. I like the lightweight feel, especially for summer. They will definitely show panty lines so thong or commando required. They’re ankle length on me so I’ll wear flats, but the waist is very elasticized and would be easy to wear a couple inches lower if you want to pair with heels. They’re high waisted (sit about an inch above my bellybutton) so good for crop tops too. But if you’re looking for tummy control or thick fabric they won’t be for you.

Size small, except in the but, that area be supersized for 2-3 buts. I jist got an average to itty bitty but, one that fits a 90lb 4' 9'er chick. Dang humunigo sweatpants! All else was good. So sad. Had to order sunthin else.

Size up with this denim shirt. I ordered a small and it fits more like an extra small. I’m 5’6”, 140#, 32D, and normally a size small in shirts for reference. The shoulders are too narrow on this shirt and I have gapping between the buttons at the bust. Also, I can’t even button the bottom two buttons at all. This shirt is just too tight on me. Oh and I also want to note that the sleeves are short when down, so they kind of have to be rolled up and buttoned. The quality is just alright. I ordered the black denim color for reference. Size was fine. fit as expected. Lots of stretch. i wear size 10 with 33 inseam. i bought 10 long sits right at ankle. Size wise, this fit like I expected. It’s not as “worn” or distressed of a denim as pictured, but would have still been fine. Otherwise the style of the jacket was good and the length was good. I’m 225lbs and 5’7” and got the Large. It fit well and still had that oversized look.

SMALL: I am 5.2 and about 130 lbs. I usually always go with a small or extra small. I ordered the small and wish I went with the XS. So comfortable exactly what I was looking for. Fits great, true to size ( a little long, aim 5'3" ) but perfect with heels Flattering Not tight Thin fabric but not sheer
So comfortable to wear great stretch as well im 5'11 - 180 lbs and ordered a size 12 they go to about my ankles and flare out on the bottom a little super cute
So comfortable! Definitely long, I’m 5’7 and ordered a small and I have to wear heels. But fabric and fit are both great

So comfortable! Id say they run big because they are so stretchy. Im usually a 12 and I got an 8. Fit perfectly. Love that it has a button and zipper with real pockets. Just like jeans but feel like leggings. Im 5’2”, 170.

So comfortable!! I’m 5’5” and very pear shaped and I think that medium fits great. It’s still hot where I live, so it’s nice to have clothes that are flowy and cool. So comfy and soft. Flattering and fun pattern. Got so many compliments dancing to Lizzo, and rocking these gems. I usually wear a medium, but read some comments about the sizing and went to a small. They fit perfect. I am 5'5 and 140 lbs. Get these for your next night out!

So comfy, so flattering, feels like quality material. I ordered 5 pair of cargo pants from Amazon and these were by far the winner. Also I’m 5’0” and the elastic draw string thing around the ankles was perfect and made it so I didn’t even have to hem the pants. 10/10 would recommend.

So comfy. So flattering. Possibly size up or order both and return one. Great quality. I ordered XL at 5’6” and 180lbs.
So cute and comfortable I got a size medium after reading the reviews. For reference I’m 5’7 and 160lbs
So cute and comfy!! I’m 5’4”, so they were a bit long, but I just wore some heeled boots to make the pants the perfect length!
So cute and comfy. I am very short. 5’0”. I wish they were shorter but they’re shorter than any other jeans I’ve tried. Super comfy. These are size 4.

So cute and great fit. 5’0”, 135lbs and a medium was perfect. I’m short but the length was fine with tall heels. I also rolled the waist to wear with flats and covered the band w a fanny pack. Highly recommend!
So cute and stretchy. I weigh 147lbs, 5’5. The size 10 fits perfectly.

So cute! So these are pretty wide legged for me as I'm 5'2", but the fit is so good at the waist and hips that I don't mind. I'm going to see if they shrink up a bit in the wash-the length as well. So far I am loving these shorts. They are lightweight, not bulky material, but not so thin that they feel cheap or show underwear lines. I am 5'2" 128 lbs, waist 29" and hips 36". I got size medium and could probably have gone with small for a more fitted pair, but based on the size chart I was a combination of small and medium measurements for waist and hips and didn't want to take the chance on it being too small/unable to zip up. Medium does not look too loose or baggy on me, but the fit is definitely looser than how many ppl would wear this style and material. Based on how they fit me and the stretchy give to the material, medium would definitely still fit someone a few pounds/several inches bigger. I do like the stretchiness to the material, but it makes me wonder if they would stretch too much after sitting in them/wearing them for an extended period of time. I haven't worn them yet except to try on, so will update this review should any of those issues occur. But so far, I'm a fan and will probably buy in more colors!

So flattering & comfortable. A little long (I am 5'1") so the needed to be hemmed but I love these pants!

So folks…listen. I’m 5ft3in. My scale says 196 naked today. It was 194 when I bought these. I’m usually 186 (been eating-cause we outside!). I can wear 12-16 or 33-35 depending on fabric. So happy with these! I am 5’10” and usually a size 10; I ordered the Large. They fit great! Love the high waist. They are cotton (not jean material) so very light and comfortable!

So I decided to get these 311 shaping skinny jeans because I have a pair of light blue 311s. I really like the way this style of Levi’s fits my figure. I am 5’4 and 187 pounds. I have no hips and a little bit of a butt. 😅. So I decided to pick the same size I already had with my first pair, which is 32x30 or a size 14. I really appreciate that the fabric is soft and stretchy . It may seem as if they are tight but I just ate. The only weird thing to me was the Levi’s label in the back of the pants. It just seemed like it was going to easily tear off.

So I did not go down in size because I wanted them loose style boyfriend jeans looking/fitting. These pants do run big but that's because their boyfriend jeans, that's the style. I do recommend going down a size if you want them slightly more fitting but they will still be some what loose I'm sure even a size down. These are not skinny jeans styled pants but I suppose if you order down in size maybe you can get that kind of fit. But if you don't want to feel uncomfortable going down sizing know your measurements for a more comfortable fit around the waist. If you look under the sizing chart for these pants it will show you waist measurements with the sizes, buy according to your waist measurement not what size you normally buy. I'm going to order another pair down a size and see how they look less looser than what I ordered because looking at the size chart according to the waist measurement I should have ordered a size down but I'm happy with the jeans I ordered because I wanted them bigger/loose anyways, more like actual boyfriend jeans fit which is exactly what I got. Update edit 10/20/23, I brought the size 1 a size down and the fit is even better, still loose in the legs and comfortable around the waist not tight but also not overly loose as the size 3 jeans. I still like the bigger one's though because I like having all different fits and styles in jeans but going down a size is more flattering to my shape yet still loose and boyfriend jeans styled still not a skinny jeans look. Also fyi I ordered the medium color denim but received the light color denim instead, at first I debated on returning because I received the wrong color but ultimately I liked them so much that I decided to keep them. But I read other review complaints about receiving the wrong color as well so just beware that you may or may not receive the right color of blue ordered.

So I found this particular jean/pant in Macy's and bought during one of their "sales". Nedless to say, the pull on feature with no zipper in front but pockets in the back are great. Definitely can dress up or down depending on what you got going on. Anyway, thought I'd look on Prime Day for these since they had a limited number in my size not realizing they also came in black jean. When I tell you these pants are THE BOMB DOT COM I am not lying! They kicked my booty up, streched in all the right places, and for someone who is 5ft 2in/5ft 3in, the inseam was PERFECT with heels from 2 inches to 3.75 inches. They are totally slimming, the rise is PERFECT (no digging if you know what I mean), the waistband is ULTRA COMFORTABLE and I have gotten compliments all over from my husband (the biggest fan) to ladies out in the stores, restaurants etc. Being a short person having a high-waist pant does help lengthen those short legs and I felt like a mini-super model with my extra long mini-legs! Do not hesistate to buy, and if you did like me and did the Try Before You Buy feature, you will definitely NOT be returning them like me and putting more in your cart. 10 STARS ALL AROUND. (Currently on "sale" at Dillards for $69.50). Get them here while they last!

So I got these and they are SO COMFY!!!! Let me just state first and foremost that I have them a 4 star review because I’m short (5ft). So they were a little long. Luckily, they don’t have sewn folds at the bottom. Otherwise, they’re cute and comfy. And for anyone reading the comments looking for what size they should order: 5ft (1.52m) 136lbs (61.69kg) I ordered a medium.

So I got this in a small, medium and large to see which size would fit me best. I am 5'5 for height and 115-120lbs(I haven't weighed myself recently to know my weight exactly). Waist is 29inches, hips is 34 inches. The size small was soo small! Size medium seemed too small because the zipper seemed too far out but because these pants are soo stretchy, it does zip up without feeling like the button is going to rip off. The size large for me was too loose feeling for my legs. The butt, it just didn't hug my body right. The size medium was the best choice for me. And let me tell you, it is soooo comfortable! My current new favorite pants for work especially! But... these are really nice pants for wearing while I am off of work too!I love that it is stretchy. I love that I can choose the length of these pants. I ordered the medium long. I love that there is a zipper too! Zipper plus stretchy and not thin quality is just super excellent!! Get this and find the right size for you!Edit: I accidentally put this in the dryer once! Oh no!!!! But actually, I still wear this for work. I am soo surprised that I can still wear it. The zipper part on the top portion of the pants remain still stretchy. My legs fit fine as well. The only difference that I could tell is the length of the pants. It did get a lil short after being in the dryer. Not high waters short, just a lil short. Still wearable for work for me. Since this product is stretchy, it does stretch back out some. I do not recommend putting these pants in the dryer though- it smelled a lil burnt.. hang dry is best.

So I hesitated on purchasing jeans online bc im short and feel like my body is an odd shape, but I am so happy with these pants. This is my first size 10 pant which made me frown, but we can thank Covid to that. The pants feel and fit so amazing. Im 5ft2in and these fall at my ankle. I got the shortest inseam which I think was 28 on the options. I weigh 149lbs and intend on losing some wait but I feel like these pants would still be good if I dropped a size or 2 bc of the stretching of the material.
