So I just had surgery so I am quite below my normal weight, but I'm 5 foot one and around 105 pounds at the moment and got extra small and they fit perfectly. They have these little plates that let the pants flare out. The Fabric is thick and looks expensive. Quality of assembly is on point. The sewing of the plaid pattern, not so much because they did not match patterns but walking into a formal meeting with a bunch of other electricians – that doesn't bother me at all. Very highly recommend. I mean, tickled with this purchase. Right up my alley

So I like these shorts ! They are not too “stiff” which I have experienced with most other jean material shorts which I have never liked (the stiff aspect) Instead, these have a nice stretch to them but without looking baggy. I will say that they are not shorts you would want to go out to eat in, for example. I consider these more for summer time going to the pool / summertime outdoor activity because they are short. But that is also society’s own problem if someone doesn’t like how short someone’s shorts are (in my opinion) so really it’s what you are comfortable with. To get them to fully fit me I had to kinda get rough with them and stretch them out a bit, but now they fit great. I am 5’3 and 104 lbs so I think they do run a bit small as it is. My pair is a size S. Overall I like these in my summertime rotation wardrobe, and just keep in mind the sizing and that they are short booty shorts 🙂

So I never EVER write reviews but I had to for these pants. So these pants are great. They are true to size. The color is nice. The distress is in a good place. The waist is comfortable. They have the right amount of stretch and loose to give that good fit. Can be casual and dress up for a night out. So for context, I am 5’5 (36, 38, 51) and squishy (per the wife 🤣) anyway I wear a lot of sneakers, flats, sandals and I am always looking for pants that hang just a little longer on my leg and touch the top of my foot down to toe’s. So I read the reviews and decided on these. Like I said they are great but I am on the fence about returning them. Being curvy it’s hard to find pants that really hang but I love them. Probably can’t put them in dryer, either way they are nice. Photos show how the pants fit. I say get them!

So I originally got an xL that’s my typical size. I’m 5ft at 175lbs the XL wasn’t flattering howeverrrr my friend got a medium, that’s what I’m wearing in the pic^^ and they fit perfect. I did cut them a little shorter bc I’m short but the flair is still perfect! 10/10 recommend! Size down!

So I read the reviews on these and wasn’t sure what size to get. I don’t have a big mid section but I do have big hips and butt so I normally wear a XL. Based on what other said about the fit I decided to go with the 3xl and they fit perfectly. If you do have a bigger mid section I could see the elastic band rolling down for those people so keep that in mind. Over all they are good quality nice material that holds you in and not see through. They are long I wore them with just a 2 inch heel and they drag about a half inch. So I would suggest if you are 5’5 or shorter wear at least a 3 to 4 inch heel. I bought these for a disco party but I also started roller skating so I will definitely be buying other colors in the future for my new hobby because the length will be perfect.

So I recently switched jobs and now have to dress business casual. As someone tall and thin 5’8.5” I have the hardest time finding pants that fit especially in the length let alone online shopping. I’ve tried the Try Before You but option (highly recommend it) on a pair similar to this so I said why not if they don’t fit I can return them. Let me just say I LOVE these!!! The color is beautiful especially during the summer with my nice tan. They were a little tight pulling up but they’re brand new so they’ll loosen up. Perfect length for me to wear with either open toe heels, sandals or the right pair of boots/booties. I’m wearing a size Medium in the turquoise. They fit great in the waist even with a little bit of fluff in my mid 30’s lol. They are mid rise which I like I can’t do high rise pants. They give me a little bit of a booty just couldn’t tell by the way I was standing. Just a suggestion for your undies to wear something smooth and possibly nude or white as you can see pattern material through the front (see circled photo).

So I was in a pinch to order shorts for my upcoming road trip honeymoon, and I made the mistake of ordering 3 of these shorts in different colors.I'm 5'6", 122 lbs, waist 26", hips 34".. small fits comfortably. The shorts are definitely stretchy and in my honest opinion, overly-frayed.. look good from the back and the side. However, the front is just sub-par. It's almost as it the shorts were cut out from men's jeans.. too much extra fabric by the crotch. Therefore, when you take a step, they bunch up in a very unflattering way. It makes it look like I have external bits, if you know what I'm saying.!Seeing as they're still comfortable and don't look toooo hideous and I simply just don't have enough time to experiment with other jeans right now, I'll keep them. But I definitely won't purchase these again.. and in fact, it's scarring me from making future Amazon clothing purchases again.

so let me start by saying that these flare pants are so glam and glitzy that they are a head-turner piece for sure. they are lined and relatively comfortable to wear. they might be a little scratchy for long-term wear, especially in between the thighs. BUT they are not short girl/petite woman friendly. these are very long pants and I will need to wear platforms when wearing them out (I'm 5'1). Otherwise, they are a few inches too long for me. also, I'm in between sizes (M-L) and ordered a Large and it barely fits me around the waist. the elastic waistband is a little snug but then the hip area is too big for me. you're going to need an hourglass (thick) figure for these to look good. I don't have hips/butt, so I'm more of a square silhouette and these look sad on me :'( make sure to look at measurements before ordering

So let me start off by saying that the first impression quality is great on these. There were a few loose threads in the zipper area, but most of those were easily pulled free and its no big deal. Moving on from that, I got the size 18 in grey twill. I usually buy 16A jeans. These jeans are very long. I admit I didnt pay attention to the inseam length as that isn't usually a problem for me being about average height (5' 5 or so). These are like 5 or 6 inches too long. See my pic, my feet are flat. I dont normally wear heels so that is an issue. Next, I think the fabric might almost be too stretchy. Or perhaps just a little too thin. I have quite a bit of a pudge in front of my stomach. Most of my jeans, even the super stretchy skinny jeans, even it out. These don't. And its very obvious. Finally these fit super well around the waist but got a bit too loose around the knees. Not much an issue but there you are. As for a huge plus! There's front pockets!🎉🎉 Im not sure if I'll return yet as they're not bad pants, just not quite right for me which is a shame because they're such a good price.

So loved the fit the feeling of the fabric and the length. I am 165 pounds , 5’2I bought the extra large fits perfect not too tight and not too loose. You can definitely dress these up or dress them down my only issue was that one of the pockets seems we’re not completely sewn .
So many compliments!! Gonna buy a few colors now. Black is classic though. I'm 5'8 and 170. A medium was perfect. Could do a large possibly. But it would be baggy.

I'm planning on getting them in size Small.I like the color and feel of the fabric, and I think they'll be a great addition to my wardrobe. In the right size.

so stinking cute and flattering! i have a small waist and a larger bottom so i was hoping these wouldn’t be too taught. they had some great flexibility and the length is perfect. i am 5’3”. i recommend!

So stretchy, fits perfect! I have a 25” waist and got the small. It fits well! Sort of short but not too short. Zipper is great quality, color is true to pic, definitely a cotton material! Very comfortable and doesn’t dig in at all!

So the crotch area is a bit loose when you pull your legs up. I'm 5 5 and 165 lbs while taking these pics. I got a large. They are a great dark denim color and the belt is a cute touch. The waist is a bit loose though so a Medium may have been more comfortable for moving around. I'll probably keep them for my more bloated days when the fit would be better.

So the cuff was just WAY too big for me. It made my legs look short, and I’m 5’9” and pretty leggy. Anyway, it was really easy to take out the stitches and make the cuff a more reasonable size to me. The jeans themselves are so stretchy and comfortable. I sized down to try to make me seem slimmer, but if I had bought my actual size, I bet I could sleep in these.

So the cut of these pants is cute, they fit as expected. But the weight of the fabric feels super heavy. It’s almost like it’s weighing the pants down. Also, I’m short but not that short and these are basically pant length on me, not cropped at all. 5’4” 122 lbs. got the small. Overall they look cute, but you would burn up if you wore them in summer.

So these are AWESOME... but mostly when standing. Sitting down, the band really crushes into your abdomen and causes some serious FUPA action. I usually wear the Express perfect lift jeans and don't have this kind of issue. The Express jeans have the thick waistband feel on the entire front to hold your mommy tummy together, but on these only the waistband is thick and holds you together, and the rest of the fabric is thinner, thus the mommy tummy getting pushed out under the waistband for some unattractive bulging. But, very cute on the butt, legs and an overall slim look, just wear with a forgiving shirt if you'll be sitting a lot. I’m a size 8, 5’3” and 145lbs.

They aren't jean material like I thought they would be, they are stretchy yoga pant material. With that being said there are no pockets or belt loops. Let me start with telling y'all I'm 5ft 10inches and 142 pounds and ended up with the medium. If you are tall and thin like me you may run into some issues with sizing. The leg length to waist ratio wasn't too great. If I got the correct size in the waist (small) I needed 2 sizes up for length. But the size that fit my length (large) are huge in the waist. So I went in the middle and bought a medium. They are a little shorter than I would like and a little baggy in the waist. Since there is no belt loops there is not much I can do with that. So for us tall ladies there is definitely an issue in sizing.

So to begin with- these are not see-through which is nice and they fit true to size. They are comfortable enough that I will wear them around the house as pajamas with a longer top but I would never leave the house in them. I'm still giving these pants 4 stars because the quality is decent and they would probably look cute on a shorter person with a different body type.

It’s a windbreaker kinda material, so there will be no stretch, only in the scrunch waistband. The length was great, had a good baggy style to it and the purple is very vibrant. It’s pretty thin too, but as expected because of the material. The pockets are great. These pants are not see-through.

So, I'm trying to switch my style and it's challenging for me to find pants. I'm 5'1, 157lbs, 40 inch hips, and 35/36 inch waist. I have a short torso and short legs haha. So, it takes a lot of courage to share this photo but it was necessary, because I care about YOU as an Amazon shopper. I need to save my fellow 5’5” or shorter ladies from a huge jean mistake. Because I don’t know who decided these were considered “mid-rise”, but my Lord, y’all. The zipper is literally longer than my hand. If you are much shorter than me, you have a strapless jumpsuit. I’ve had a few pairs of Wranglers in the past for riding. And usually they are my go to because they do last. But these will be sent back and the hunt continues for a decent pair of jeans.Side Note: I got Davis, 9x32. The denim is actually really nice, I liked the back pocket detail and the length was great. So, let me start off by saying they don’t look as good in the pictures as I feel they do in real life. I am 5’9” and between 175-180 depending on the week of the month 🙄. Anyway! I LOVE these. It’s so hard for me to find pants that don’t give me camel toe because one of my hips is a little higher up than the other. But these pants fit so great. I thought they’d be a little longer, but I really don’t need to wear heels at all with these. I actually have to wear them a little lower than I wanted because they don’t go as long as I’d like - I wanted them to sweep the floor honestly. So, my waist is 33”. Since I didn’t have any option to choose a size, I chose 33” waist. I am usually a size 9. These jeans are a 16. I am beyond confused. So fyi, 33” jeans mean 38” waist.

So, the plus is that I believe these fit TTS. I am 5'6" and usually wear an XL and I purchased the XL. They fit around my waist perfectly. They are a pretty heavy true denim and would be good for cooler months. The downside... I am not sure these would look flattering on *anyone*. They kinda balloon out at the knee, which I know is the trend for "horse shoe" jeans, but these are just... not good. I really wanted to love them but I don't think they are for me.

So, these jeans don’t barrel as much as others I’ve tried. But they are a dream to wear. They feel comfortable and ready for anything. I agonized over the measurements before ordering. I have a 27“ true waist near my belly button (I measured). I am normally a size 24 jeans. I an 5“4“ and 110 lbs. I ordered the XS. It doesn’t sit at my high waist. It sits on my hips. The bottoms graze my feet with an extra inch pooled in fabric at the bottom. If I tried these on at the store, I would purchase based on comfort, i am acknowledging that the price point is high for a brand I’m not familiar with. They are sewn well and made with high quality in mind.

So, these were almost a "miss" for me. The stress lines in the front just weren't realistic and look odd. But, everything else was perfect. They are true to size. I got the small and the fit and length is perfect (I'm 5'4" 120lbs, waist 27 hips 35). The high waist rests at a high but comfortable location. The flare is a perfect size for today's trend. I like the weight of the material, light yet sturdy. Since everything else was good, I decided to try to fix the distress lines. I used a black fabric pen and filled them in somewhat. And, it turned out great. Problem solved. So, now I'm quite happy with the pants and will wear them frequently.

So. I’m a wimp and like to wear leggings under my regular pants during the colder months, I’ll order a medium next time to accommodate for that. The small DOES fit with the leggings but they’re a little snug, like if I went to work bloated, I would definitely be uncomfortable. Without the leggings though? They’re fine. I’m 5’3 and 155 lbs. I plan on ordering a few more pairs in different colors!

Some of the other reviews said the small was huge. They must be teeny tiny! I am a 6 and have some junk in the trunk and they fit like a glove. I am 5’3” and 140 pounds for reference.

Sorry about my super grubby mirror and messy floor. The pants feel high quality and are comfy, but I’m apple shaped so only very high waisted pants tend to work for me. Starting with the dislikes, this skirt took over a month to arrive. The original order got lost in the mail so they sent me a new one. The skirt was packaged inside a vacuum sealed plastic bag. Upon opening it, there was a slight odor to the skirt material itself. The color of the fabric was vibrant. I ordered a small. I am 5’6” and 130lbs. I was able to walk my normal strides and pace comfortably. The skirt did not place any restrictions to my legs walking. The buttons are all functional, you can undo as many as you want or none at all. Overall, I wanted a nostalgic fashion piece, and this Jean skirt was it. My teen daughters both really liked it on me and want to borrow it themselves. Despite the delivery time issues, it was an okay purchase over all. But I wouldn’t buy from this company again.

STATS: 5’4” 130 lbs Tops: small, 36 B Bottoms: small/medium, size 6 pants Dresses: small, size 4 I’m always on the hunt for a good doop. I wanted the Abercrombie tailored shorts look for less and found these and figured I’d give them a try. I originally ordered a small but the waist was a bit too snug. I returned and purchased a medium and they are perfect! They are fitted but comfortable. The fabric is high quality. Not too thick, not too thin. I ordered the beige color and they are not see through. I washed them on delicate and laid flat to dry and there was no shrinkage. Will be ordering in black.

Stats: 5’6”, 145lbs, 27in waist, 40.5in hips (I workout but I enjoy cookies) These fit well. They are not denim…more like “denim” like workout shorts. Very stretchy and fit like a second skin. My thighs are the thickest part of my body. I need bottoms that accommodate my thighs first and usually always have to get the waist taken in. These would likely stretch out as I wore them and get baggy in the waist. I might consider a small over a medium, but the chance it would not fit my thighs becomes the issue. I’ll never know. Probably helped that they’re bootcut. The material felt so soft. Like a jean/ legging hybrid. I’m too lazy to send them back but these will make a great Christmas present for a family member. Thanks to corticosteroids I've gained weight fast so I needed new shorts ASAP and for the price these work great! Comfortable, stretchy, dark blue wash color with rolled edge and deep front pockets. They sit right at my belly button. I'm 5'2" and 180-200 lb.

The - Blooming Jelly Women Linen Pants Summer Vacation Outfits Wide Leg High Waisted Pants Palazzo Flowy Pants Trouser 2024 – are fun and frilly pants that make me happy when wearing them. I love the way the fabric feels on my skin and moves when I walk. They’re not the most flattering style of pants for my fuller figure but I don’t care - I like them. The only somewhat negative comment I have is the sizing chart lists the measurements of the pants themselves and not the body. And they don’t seem to be that accurate in my opinion. I ordered the size XL which according to the chart the hip measurement is 42”. When I measured the pants I received while flat, I measured 52 inches at the hip. The X-Large does fit me but if I would like the pants to look on me as they do on the model, more roomy and gathered like a skirt under the elastic yoke area, then I should have ordered a larger size or two, if it were available. Regardless of all that, these pants are lovely and I’m very glad I chose to purchase them. For sizing reference, my Bust Measures 44”, my waist measures 37”, and my hips measure 47”. I’m 5’4” tall. I purchased a size XL.

The - GRAPENT Wide Leg Jeans for Women Trendy High Waisted Flare Stretchy Bell Bottom Denim Pants Baggy Raw Hem Trouser Jeans – are not on my list of favorite jeans. For starters, they have a strong chemical smell that after a few days left to air out, is still very strong. I cannot wait to launder them. Before I do that, I want to cut from the hem at least 2 inches if I intend to wear with heels. If I wear flats, I’ll need to take off about 3 or 4 inches. I’m 5’4” tall. The fit is decent in that these jeans are not too tight nor are they too big. However, because the fabric is extremely stretchy, these pants keep falling down…and I can put them on and take them off without the need to unbutton and unzip. I’m hoping that after they are laundered, this will be less of an issue. Overall, I don’t think this style of jeans is well suited to my fuller figure frame and I would not buy these again. For sizing reference, my waist measures 37”, and my hips measure 48”. I purchased a size XXL.

The - Sidefeel Women's Jeans 2024 High Waist Bootcut Jeans Fashion Stretchy Jeans for Women – are very figure flattering for my fuller figure frame however, the cut of the pants through the legs and torso is too long for my short in height stature. They certainly do not look on me the same way they do on the model in the listing photos which leads me to believe that perhaps these jeans are better suited for the tall and thin. Nonetheless, these jeans are wearable with a pair of platform shoes or high heels. Maybe they'll shrink a little with washing and fit me better. For sizing reference, my waist measures 38”, and my hips measure 48”. I am 5'4" tall. I purchased a size 18.
The 25s are so much smaller than the ones I have bought before. I am 5’5”, 115lbs, 24” waist. I will have to return.

The advertised picture looks nothing like what was sent. First I ordered size large but XL was sent. Second, the picture shows a very straight leg crop fit but the actual is wide leg and super long. Realized that I'm only 5'2 so I didn't expect them to be too crop on me but did expect them to at least touch the top of my shoe. I'm definitely returning them

The Angel's Forever Young 360 Bootcut Jeans are perfect for my everyday lifestyle. I purchased these jeans as I have gained weight and all my other jeans cut into my waist and had less give than these jeans. The waistband and denim are soft and thick with enough stretch for comfort without losing its shape. After all we all need a little support when you are as curvy as I am. These do not change your shape like butt lifting jeans do. I will be doing more squats for that...lol. I would buy these again in a different color. I bought a size 6. My measurements are waist 37.5", booty 44", hips 43.5" and inseam 28.5". I could hem them, but I like that my ankles stay warm when I squat.

The Belle Poque High Waisted Denim Skirt with Belt in the Color: “Black Denim” is Fantastic!!! I ordered size Small and the fit is excellent! For reference I’m 5’2”, short torso, and weigh 113 pounds. I love this denim skirt with belt! It’s comfortable to wear, flattering, it has a nice thickness to the denim and nice stretch as well. It’s stylish. I really like the style with the front pockets design. I really like the length. I’m so glad I found this pretty denim skirt! I definitely recommend it…

The belt color is a little reddish than brown, with a burnt looking finish on the edges, but I really like the look.q Just be aware if you are trying to match colors with other true browns on a bag or your shoes. I got this for a 44" waist pant wearer and the belt barely fits. I'm glad I went with the bigger size as this one is in the very last hole. The belt comes with a punch so you can add more holes if you lose or gain weight. Using the last hole on the belt the end just barely tucks into the next belt loop. Overall I still really like the belt.

The belt is very pretty and the leather is thick. I ordered the 22“-24“ for my 24“ waist and I can wear it on the second hole. It fits just right. Upon receipt, the leather has some creases in it. Hopefully this is a one off on my specific cut of leather. If I was buying this at a store, I’d still buy this belt, I do like everything else about it, but I’d search for a different one, without a crease.

The belts are the size that was advertised and fit great. I am a 25 inch waist but I love how you can adjust them larger or smaller depending on how high or low on your waist you want them. Then you can also loop them through and kind of tie the end if you’re wearing them with a dress which is nice The colors came out lovely and as you can see, the quality is great. I think the actual gold buckle part looks just a little cheap maybe because of the tint, but it’s not a big deal. Really glad I got these, and I would definitely recommend!

The belts are the size that was advertised and fit great. I am a 25 inch waist but I love how you can adjust them larger or smaller depending on how high or low on your waist you want them. The best thing about these work pants - the pockets!!!The fabric is super soft. Ther eis room in the stomach to grow (I'm 6mo). The pockets are large and have a lot of room. I am 5'-6" and these pants are a little too long. Invest in some fabric tape, a tailor or a sewing machine!

The black linen pants were selected for a woman 5'8" tall, 168lb. Based on the sizing charge, size Large was selected and they are a good fit. They are ankle length...not calf. The waist is elastic all the way round and there is a tie string. I like the attractive aesthetic buttons down the leg....they add interest. The linen fabric is not thin and flimsy...pretty substantial. The seams are well sewn, and I see no significant issues with quality. I like them.

The buckle was very obviously rose gold not yellow gold as shown in the image. See comparison to my yellow gold ring. Also the cream/white colored belt was more tan/beige than white. Quality is a bit questionable but nice for the price. I got the large and I have a 34” waist so it’s true to size.

The color and fabric of these cargo pants is amazing. The garment construction is very well done and it’s just super unique to have a pair of cargo pants that are made of corduroy. I thought that these would be looser fitting and they actually are and I should’ve ordered a size up. I am typically between a medium and large and these mediums look a little tight. They are by no means too tight to wear, but I would’ve liked a loser flowing fit, and gone with a large. They are plenty long enough for even my 5’9” frame. The pockets are super functional without being bulky and the fabric is nice and thick. Even though they do appear to be a little tight on me, they are not at all uncomfortable.

The color and material of these reminds me or elastic ankle jeans from the 90s. I ordered “navy” expecting a deep blue cotton and instead it’s a faded blue and thin denim. You can see how far off they are from the photo. They feel cheap and look awful. Maybe other colors are better. I’m returning them. Sizing is about true to size— perhaps they run just a bit small. I bought a large and I’d say they fit me. I’m an 8/10 in clothes and wear either a medium or large depending on fit. 5’5 150lbs

The color combination (burgungy and white) of this sweater is very nice and goes well with jeans as well as leggings. It is slightly loose fitting that I will able to wear a shirt underneath, should I have a need for days when the weather is cold and hot. The hem can be folded in and the sleeves pushed up, without slipping down. The sweater was much softer after washing and laying flat to dry and no stiching became unraveled. It is a well made sweaterI ordered the small based on the size chart they provide. I am 5ft, 120-125lbs.

The color is described initially as Blush Pink. For reference I have photographed the pants on a Blush Pink quilt. Not even close to a Blush Pink. In ordering, the color is described as Coral Pink. Closer, but I would say Salmon Pink is a correct descriptor. It’s okay, Salmon Pink will work into the autumn, so maybe it’s even better. But I did order three tops to go with Blush Pink pants, and thus is disappointing. I took a star off for that reason. I ordered size 12 Long and the fit is perfect (5’7” 163 lb). It’s a thicker denim than in other leggings from Amazon Essentials, more like regular denim. Still has stretch. I think I will get a lot of wear from them.

The color is exactly as pictured. I'm 5'8" so I ordered an XXL to make sure it hung correctly. The material is soft and did not gather when I walked or stood up/sat down. It's not thin or to thick and definitely worth the price.

The color is great. I like the material. It's stretchy but not leggings stretchy so it kinda keeps things tight. I bought 2 sizes. I'm keeping both because they both fit. I'll probably wear the smaller ones for going out and the bigger ones more for everyday. My measurements 5'6", 136 lbs, 32DD, 29 waist, 39 hip, 12 inch rise - crotch to belly button and I've got big thighs, as you can see:) oh and as other reviews stated they are weirdly baggy at the knees but I can live with it.

The color is more of a mustard yellow than the vibrant yellow I was expecting but still a fun look for spring. They are SUPER comfy, a cotton feel, they are like you’re wearing pajamas almost. Personally for me I felt like they made my hips look large, I’m not sure why. Maybe the pleats? And I wish they were longer. I’m 5’9 and have a hard time getting the look of the product shots that the pant hits the floor. All in all a fun pant if you want a comfy look but if you’re going for super chic not these.

The color is more of a mustard yellow than the vibrant yellow I was expecting but still a fun look for spring. They are SUPER comfy, a cotton feel, they are like you’re wearing pajamas almost. The color of the denim is lighter blue, not the darker blue as pictured. I picked MEDIUM size from their size chart. I should have gotten a LARGE. The pants do have some stretch to them. They are pretty tight. Scared to dry them and they will shrink. I’m 5’5 about 145 weight and large would be a fit next time.

The color on the sweatpants are super cute and just like the image a bright pink. They are thick material and the waistband is a thick waistband. I like these sweatpants but at times they feel a bit thick for my liking. I like in a warm climate. For those in a cooler climate these would be perfect! The fit is true to size as I am 5’3 around 140lbs and I ordered a medium they were perfect fitting.Overall, cute color, great fit, and comfortable.

The color was right on with the image. The pants are a slightly thicker jegging like material. Form fitting and moveable! I am a midi size so I need plus’s size hips but a form fitting waist and this absolutely did it! No baggyness on the crotch and not too tight on the legs. The button does seem a bit fake like a child’s plastic snap but it’s covered by my shirt anyways. JUST BUY THE PANTS! I wear a size 2XL am 5”7

The color was slightly off from what was pictured. But overall they are cute! Elastic/ stretchy waist band and fit shirt. I am 5”6 around 155 pounds and wearing a medium, but probably should have ordered a large!

The denim fabric of the - FTCayanz Women's Baggy Jeans Pull On Denim Joggers Elastic Waist Casual Harem Pants with Pockets – is very soft making these pants very comfortable to wear. The fabric is on the thin side so the pants fall and drape nicely. The color of the denim fabric, in person seems to be darker with less fading than it appears in the listing’s photographs. Not a deal breaker, it’s just that I would have liked more fading. The fit is great. The length is long for my 5’4” frame but I like the cuffed up look. The waistline pockets are roomy but the only back pocket, is small. I would have preferred two larger back pockets. Interesting looking aglets (see attached photo). Overall, these are cute and comfy baggy joggers and I like them a lot. For sizing reference: My Waist Measures 38", My Hips Measure 48”. I ordered the size 2XL

The denim is really nice weight in these high waist jeans. No visible panty lines, they’re not so heavy that they are hot to wear and have just enough stretch for them to keep their shape. The seams and hems are uniformly stitched and sturdy also. The legs are very wide, as shown, but I don’t feel that they have a baggy fit elsewhere. Unfortunately, on my body shape and height I don’t find them to be at all flattering I don’t know if it is the back pocket placement, the leg length or the snug fit of the jeans from the waist to the hips. (I’m 5’4” tall and wearing a size 10.) Even with a 2”heel I felt I looked short and squat. Not a look I can successfully carry off. On a more slender, taller frame they would be very cute.

The design is so cute, reminds me of my childhood! The length is nice on me, I’m between 5’6” 5’7”. I wish the fabric had a little more stretch to it though because I really could barely fit it over my butt, but I made it work. I am 195 lbs and ordered an XL which is my usual size.

The Dokotoo Womens Relaxed Fit High Waist shorts are fantastic! They look and fit perfectly! I absolutely love the feel of the cotton fabric (not sheer at all) and the elastic waist. So comfy! I already know these shorts will be favorite piece this summer! I wear Large (for a loose look and extra comfy fit), I am 5'7" and 142 pounds.

The Dokotoo Womens Relaxed Fit High Waist shorts are fantastic! They look and fit perfectly! I absolutely love the feel of the cotton fabric (not sheer at all) and the elastic waist. So comfy! I already know these shorts will be favorite piece this summer! I wear Large (for a loose look and extra comfy fit), I am 5'7" and 142 pounds.

The dress is loose and comfortable, very soft the color is exactly as shown in the picture and I received a lot of compliments from this dress. I am five 1 1/2 and I weigh 127. I like it so much I have just ordered the black and the white hopefully I’ll be just as happy with those.

The dress was fine, but not exactly what I had in mind. The dark denim color is more of just a navy blue, at least it looked that way to me. I got a small and I’m 5’2” about 130 with a 36B/C. Fit fine except for a bit of puckering at the chest when I moved my arms a certain way, also a bit of puckering at the lower buttons with some movement. Decided I’d send it back.
The dress was super comfy. I’m 5’9” and it was on the shorter side but nothing unmanageable. Received many compliments. Wish it was a little cheaper but I would definitely buy it again.

The elastic waistband makes them super comfy. I ordered a L short initially, but had to return for the L regular length as the short was too short for the fit I wanted. I am 5’3”, 145lbs. My one complaint is that there is no button, only a metal clasp— I am expecting this to fall off pretty quickly, especially if you have to sit for a long time in these. I will add a button myself! Will likely order another color.

The fabric and color are great. However it pulls across the tummy if you have any fluff below the belly button and will fold up on the waistband if you have muscle in your backside. I am a suggested Amazon M and I’m exchanging for an XL to wear with drapes camisoles and jardigans for work, travel, or anything where I need to look out together without anything binding, clingy, or that I would have to peel off of sweaty skin. Camisole is quince M, bra size 36ddd freya, Levi straus high rise shorts size 8 to give perspective on exactly how this would work for you. Tank size M/L.

The fabric and construction are very nice on these pants. Made in Bangladesh from 95% Cotton, 5% Elastane, the fabric is a decent thickness, the buttons are well sewn and it even has a full metal YKK zipper. The fit is where these lost stars for me. They are super tight in the waist on me. Really they are tight all over but they are most uncomfortable in the waist. The length is ok on me, in fact if I uncuff them I could even wear them as a full length pant if they were not so tight! For size reference I am 5'4" 150lbs, pear shaped and typically wear a size 8 or 10 in pants. In the picture I am squeezed into the size 8 in black. I don’t even think the size 10 would work. These pants are also very soft and have a brushed feel to them, in black they attract lint like crazy, so consider a lighter color or keep a lint roller handy at all times!

The fabric and quality seem great. I am between 5’4 and 5’5 and weigh around 140 pounds but have larger thighs and hips. I got a medium with 33” inseam. The medium works for me but if you have a belly I would recommend sizing up. My hips are wide enough I could probably also fit a large. I do not have a belly but these seem slightly tight in the waist in the medium. Next time I will buy 31” inseam as they are a little long. Definitely buying more colors.

The fabric feels sturdy and stretches well. It didn’t shrink after washing. I love the color. On someone taller, these are probably great. Being 5ft tall, the rise fits more like high rise vs mid. The waistband comes to my bellybutton, which makes them look a bit awkward on me. Not the fault of the jeans, just frustration for me and my endless search for clothes. If not for that, these would have been perfect.

The fabric has a proper thickness and weight that makes the pants look very luxurious. I am 5’5” and 142 lbs. A large fit me very loosely and makes me look bigger. Maybe a medium would have worked better for me. It is a very looking pair of pants.

The fabric honestly wasnt bad, would be great for summer. Cute pajama style but they do not fit like the picture. I was hopeful, but unfortunately returning. I would also order a size larger than original size, length is full length not cropped. Im 5’2”

The fabric in these pants is actually thick. It's not see through and has a pretty good quality feel for polyester. The color is spot on as well. Unfortunately, they are oversized throughout and look VERY weird in the front - see my pics. At 5'5, 130 lbs I got an M. Probably should have gone with an S, but I made this decision based on my waist size and the fact that one reviewer before me, at about the same size and weight, mentioned she wished she would have ordered an M instead of the S. Well, the M doesn't really work either. The manufacturer should consider using elastic at the back, so this item could fit more people.

The fabric is a cotton jersey material... Which isn't my favorite, look wise, but man! Are these suckers COMFY!!! I'm 5'2" and they're a tad long but you can cinch them pretty high if you want. They hang compactly so you don't really see the full billow effect... But they're sure cozy!

The fabric is a good quality and fit is as expected based on reviews. My suggested and normal size is a Small, but I chose a size Medium due to the comments around length. I am 5'6 and 140 lbs for reference. These are too short for me to wear heels.
The fabric is cheap and kind of itchy. They’re super cute on but not soft and they run short. I am 5’3 132lbs sz4 and they are barely grazing the ground. Just not really work $39

The fabric is nice, not overly sheer. The back of the pants are flattering on my rear, which is usually my problem area.. The linings from the front pockets show thru more than I would like. But mainly the pants are just made way too short, which is why I am returning them. I ordered size small, am 5’6, 118 pounds. Usually wear a 2 or 25 in pants. they probably needed to be about 3 inches longer.

The fabric is pretty good for the money. I wish the waist was slightly tighter and the thighs were slightly looser. I’m 5’2”, 115 lb and athletic. I got the small. The thighs and butt seem a little too tight to wear to work, yet the waist is about 1 inch too loose. I also think the waist should be lower (low rise?) because it sits at a weird spot that makes the fabric bunch up. Overall they are good for the money. They are great from the knee down. Good length for petite women and comfortable except the extra fabric in the waist/hips.

The fabric is stretchy but has a good weight. They don't start tight and end up too loose at the end of the day which I love. Real pockets, cute wash, not too short- they go just below the ankle (I'm 5'6"). I feel like these cover the mom pooch pretty well. I got a large and I like the amount of room they have. Overall better than I expected.
The fabric is stretchy, but it is also snug. Can be pulled up to make it higher-waisted. 5’4”, 125 lbs, size small

The fabric of these pants are a little bit cheap, but they’re definitely thicker than I thought they were going to be so that’s nice. And they aren’t see-through which, of course I love. So I’m on the fence about the quality, but the fabric does have this slightly fuzzy texture, I’m not sure how else to put it. But most importantly, I actually think they look pretty good on, and they are true bellbottoms as opposed to being called bellbottoms, and actually being boot cut, lol. They aren’t particularly versatile, because they are a very specific style, but I’m digging it. I think they are in fact pretty cool, and I do like the era and vibe, so I’m actually looking forward to wearing them and seeing what other people think! Lol. I think these are a size medium and I am 5’5” 125 lbs 32-27-34. They are a little tight around my stomach right now but that’s because I’m about 10 pounds heavier than I normally am so I think when I am back in shape soon, these will fit perfectly! I would certainly recommend them if you are also into this late 60s era of clothing because the pattern and the colors are spot on. I hope this review is and you have a fantastic day!
The fabric of these pants are super soft but they are not long enough. I'm 5'11 and usually wear 36 in inseam. So these had to be returned.
The fabric of these pants are super soft but they are not long enough. I'm 5'11 and usually wear 36 in inseam. So these had to be returned.

The fabric these jeggings are made of is thick enough to look like real jeans, but just a soft and stretchy as leggings. Way better quality than most jeggings. I got the black and the dark wash and I really like both of them. I love having no button to cut into my tummy when I sit, or bulky zipper under my shirts and sweaters. I'm 5'2" and 110lb and the 0 fits snugly but isn't too tight to sit cross legged. The 0 is likely long enough to cover your ankles if you are up to 5'6" or so, but for shorter legs it's easy to just tuck the extra fabric under for a hemmed look. These are likely to become my go-to jeans to wear with sneakers or boots. I wish they made them in boot cut, too!

The feel the exact same as Buckles jeans. Only thing I wish was different, would be if the jeans were tapered in more. Otherwise worth every penny for the price. I’m 5’4, 155 lbs and bought a size 5X36 (36in long)

The first pair I bought broke immediately upon trying on. I thought about simply returning but I really liked the fit and style so I ordered a replacement. The second pair has been washed and dried and hasn’t broken (yet). I would hope for the price that the quality is good, only time will tell. I’m 5’ 4” 140lb and I bought a medium.

The first thing I noticed about these jeans (Bb002-blue) were how comfortable they are and how good the rips at the bottom hem will look with heels or boots. Also, how darn baggy they are on me, especially in the thighs. My thighs are only 18” around so I’m used to this. Drying in the machine helped to shrink them a bit and make them wearable for me, although now they’re kinda short. I should have gotten a size 2, but there’s none. For reference, I’m 5’7”, 27”Waist, 34”Hips, usually a 1 or 3 in AE jeans. A comfy fit, the waistband isn’t too tight. But the back pockets are a bit on the large size, making my butt look even smaller. That being said, I’ll mention some other flaws I noticed. Nothing major. The “creases” where the thigh bends are clearly very fake looking. Not like they’re genuinely used and distressed. I was also disappointed to find the style isn’t quite flared. It’s more boot-cut, the flare being overly exaggerated in their pictures. Still, they look okay. They don’t tend to keep their shape either and loosen while wearing (as did all the ae denim). I may need to keep dry-shrinking these, as they do loosen with wear, but they’re coming to fit nicely and I think I’ll add them to my regular jean cycle. Chances are, you’ve got more curves to fill these jeans out, so they’ll fit and look much better than they do on me. That being the case, I’d recommend these for sure, especially at this value.

the first thing you notice is how soft these are! model is 5'2" 105lbs wearing size 4. wish they were a size 2 but the 4 fits a bit oversized and comfortable so no complaints. they have a nice amount of stretch to them and are properly high waisted. the wash is very fashionable and well done. they are a longer leg so if you need a shorter leg be prepared to cut them.

The fit and comfort were definitely on point; loose and flowy. However, they did not look frumpy. I usually size up for length, and I am not sure that was necessary. As a 5'9" girl, this was huge. Too bad I had to return them to the seller. Unfortunately the low rating is all about transparency. In the image attached to this review, the silhouette of my hand is on the right and the rectangle retail tag to the right of the drawstring. They were visible in indirect, natural light standing in my office. I kept the army green pair purchased in the same order, because the transparency was less evident.

The fit is a little weird. Im 5'3 and fluctuate between 128-135. I got the 121 Little flower in a small. Material: The fabric isn't as stretchy and the feel is not flowy or soft. You definately feel the synthetic polyester against your skin. Since its a bit stiff you can't really move or do a lunge comfortably without feeling strain. The Cut: The top half is about 11.5 in. from crotch to waistband. I like high waist bottoms but if you have a short torso I wouldnt recommend it. Sure you can fold it over but the top garter which is very tapered has no give and it will bite into any soft spots in your tummy area. Any bulges the fabric will stretch and thin showing a lighter slightly see through spot. The pant leg is very long. 29" inseam and looks more like a straight pant leg than a bell bottom flow. Sure you can hem it, but you will lose any slight bell bottom shape. Design: The print is beautiful which is why I gave it more stars than I would have otherwise. The color is bright and crisp an no bleed throughs. My only gripe is that the print is not flattering to my butt. Im not sure if this makes sense but the print has a more of a camouflage effect to make things bumps appear smaller. This might also have something to do with the cut falling straight down from the hip instead of your thigh like a bell bottom should. I think even if I size up the cut will still be wierd and any hemming I can do will just make the pants boxier. I want to give it a shot because of the pretty print but its just not what I was looking for so I will be return it.

The fit is excellent, feels so good, and great for my 5ft frame!!

The fit is perfect! I am 5’2-1/2” and weigh 116 pounds. I could not tell from the size chart which size to order so I started with the small....and then had to return for an extra small. Will be wearing to a 60’s dance and probably after that as well. The embroidery is beautiful and the pants are somewhat light weight. Wearing them with a cork wedge heel and will not need to shorten. Brings me right back to the 60’s and feel like a flower child!
The fit is perfect!!! The material is soft, flexible and the right amount of thickness. I ordered small petite. My measurements 28 waist & 37 hips.
The fit is true to size. For reference, I’m 5’8” and 155 lbs. I bought size large and it’s perfect. The waist is accurate and fit is very comfortable. The pockets are usable. I recommend these pants!
The fit of these pants were great, I wish they were a few inches longer so I could comfortably wear them with heels! I’m 5’7” by the way. Size XL is what I ordered.

The fit of these wide-leg jeans is outstanding! I am 5’4” 138 lbs, 30” waist. I bought the Large 27” and these required NO alterations. The length is perfect, can wear flat sandals or tennis shoes. They give a pillar effect, they make me look taller. They are so comfortable and airy. The fabric is wonderful, heavy, real denim. I went with the Dark Blue, which looks great. The style is reminiscent of my favorite jeans I had in college back in the 70s.

The fit on these pants is much better than I expected... they don't cling right to my hips and butt like a lot of pants like this do; they're just a tiny, tiny bit looser. That being said, you still need to order the same size you normally would. I'm 5'8" and 155, and wear a size 12. Pardon the belly in the pic...🤷‍♀️ The fabric of these pants is very nice... not too thick or thin so they can be worn year-round. Excellent quality. They came out of the dryer beautifully. The only tiny little complaint I have for these is that while the waistband appears to be about 3 inches wide, the elastic band inside is only about 1/2 inch wide, and I can already tell I'll have an issue with it rolling and twisting as time goes on. It would be nice if it was about 2 inches wide. I think it would feel more secure around the waist also, although it's fine now.

The fit was as expected. I’m 5’2 125lbs with a mom pouch. These fit well everywhere but in the ankles. They are about 3 inches too long and bag. I ordered Size 7 Short. For the price I can’t complain too much. They feel like real jeans. Wish they were actually the correct length for my body. They also had a strange smell, can’t wear right out of the bag...they will need to be washed to get the smell off.

The fit: I'm 5'4 180lbs with a 37 inch waist. I ordered a 2xl according to the size chart provided by the seller. The pants were a few sizes too big. I think the waist band size stated is the smallest that the band gets, the pants I ordered were very stretchy. The tag also said they were a size 20/22 when I normally wear a 16. The look: Not that dressy. I wasn't expecting professional slacks but I was expecting something above pajama pants. They remind me a little of scrubs, but less functional.

THE GOOD: —Nicely packaged. —Hose are sheer —Husband said they looked good —Elastic top —Reinforced toe —Top will stretch to 9” (with effort) —They were appropriate length (to my knee) —Colors were as expected Sounds good so far!! But… THE BAD: —Elastic top is REALLY snug —Uncomfortable after 20 minutes wear —After 35 minutes, I had to take them off because of the pain around the top. Felt like a rubber band around my leg. I’m 5’6” and weigh 194. I actually thought these knee highs would be too large, if anything. Nope! They are definitely not plus size. Remember, the gals in the advertising look like they’re happy as can be. I’ll bet in reality, they couldn’t wait to get done with the shoot so they could get circulation back in their lower legs. Bahaha!! Unfortunately I cannot in good conscience recommend these knee highs to plus size gals. They may be great, however for smaller ladies.

The GRAPENT Wide Leg High Waisted Stretchy Cropped Jeans with Pockets in the Color: “Darkness Blue” are Awesome! I ordered size small and the fit is great as you can see in the pictures that I included. For reference I’m 5’2”, short torso and weigh 113 pounds. The fit is comfortable and the fabric has a nice stretch to it. This jeans are flattering and stylish. I really like the design and cut. I’m so glad I found these!!! I definitely recommend that you get them…

The gray color of the pants is very nice. The weight is surprising - the material is heavier than I expected. The waist fit is accurate to my size but the length is made for a tall woman (5'8" and above). At 5'7", I can only wear these slacks with very high heels.These pants are not designed to flatter one's figure. They lay straight down without any contour or styling. The sewing craftmanship is good. Overall I would give the quality an 8 out of 10 and the fashion design a 6 out of 10.

The Green All-in-One Plus Size arrived sooner than expected with Amazon Prime.This Plus Size one piece runs small in the tummy, hips and thigh areas. It is designed for someone with a long turso and height over 5'4". The sleeves and legs of the one piece are long, if you have short arms and legs, it gathers well.It is not designed for anyone owning a foopah. It is not designed for that shape.The one piece is beautiful, the color is as pictured and it is not sheer, so there is no see through the material. However, there is no give in the outfit and to be comfortable for sitting and removing if you have to go to the bathroom requires you to size up 1 size for comfort and flexibility. I purchased a 2x, I am between 221 and 241. I am about 5'2". It does not flatter my pear shape, petite body type, however, I will try to make it work because it is very pretty.Wore it Today at Church for Palm Sunday. It was a HIT. Folks actually touched it to feel the material, it is gorgeous. I will be passing this jumpsuit on to someone who has the height and shape for it. Really nice jumpsuit, runs slim in the torso, stomach and hips area. However, it is rather flattering on the body. I did not wear the belt, I could not find it after having this in the closet for so long. The belt was not missed.

I hope the pictures that I included with my review are to you. These pants look Elegant, well made, beautiful sheen, nice length, great style and design. The fabric feels of good quality (not too thin), and the material feels comfortable and breathable.

The High Waist Straight Leg Tummy Control Jeans (ASIN: B0CTH4DY8Y) are a good fit as they fit as expected. At 5’6” I found these jeans are finally long enough, something I struggle with. The material has a nice, non-binding stretch. The pockets are well placed, but a bit shallow. The color is good for blue jeans. Overall, they are a comfortable pair of blue jeans.

The item description states that the inseam for regular is 31". I just received my item and the tag states they are regular but the inseam is 32". I was already pushing it with the 31" inseam because I am a shorty however the short inseam I think would be too short but I'm wondering what's the true inseam is on the short 🤔.

The jeans are a great quality super thick, beautiful color, but also super tight. Maybe I should’ve went over the reviews a little bit better as some people did say they did go up a size. For me I probably should’ve went up two sizes. In my mind I always think I’m a size 10 maybe a size 12, with these pants I probably should’ve gotten a 14. They do go super high all the way up to the small part of your waist so the part around your waist has no give no stretch. The part around your thighs your booty your legs is super stretchy, so it was hard to think what size only because I didn’t want around my legs or my booty to be super big. They are a wide legged jeans which isn’t my favorite but I honestly don’t mind these. Especially for the winter or colder months with some cute boots or maybe some UGGs. I would’ve liked for the waste part maybe to stretch a little bit more to provide a little bit more comfort. As I said they do go super high up to the smaller part of your waist almost right under your rib cage so they are super tight and there’s no give up there. I may wear these once or twice I’m kind of a little scared to wash them. I don’t want them to get smaller or tighter. I am 5‘,4“ and for how high they go they were right at my ankle so if you are taller and they do go that high up your waist they’re gonna be very short. I feel a little 50-50 on these. Like I said the gene the stretch is very nice. Nice color, but I find them too tight maybe a little too uncomfortable for a lot of wear. Maybe for a nice outing or date I can totally wear these. I’m not very happy of how high they go only because I’m not very thin and I feel like that smaller part of your stomach at the bottom Does show a lot so a longer sweater or a longer jacket will definitely be my choice.

The jeans are nice but the sizing is tricky. I am wearing size XL with a 32" inseam. I usually wear size 12/14 and I would call these jeans size 16 based on how they fit. They fit comfortably in the waist and hips and they are just a little bit loose but I don't mind that. The problem is the length. I am wearing boots with 2" heels and I had to roll a double cuff to keep the the legs from dragging on the ground. After I write this review, I'm going to hem up these pants. Is this style cute on me? My legs are long and I usually wear skinny jeans or leggings. I think baggy jeans make my legs look shorter. It's a matter of personal taste if you like the look.

The jeans are not as light-colored as I had expected, but I put them on and fell in love! They fit just the way I'd hoped - fitted to the knee then flared out. Fits like a damn glove! I usually wear 10, and the 9 fits wonderfully. I'm 5'6" and 150. Order them!

The jeans are perfect. I’m 5’4” 200lbs and bought a 14 plus and they fit perfect. I’m wearing 3 inch heels so without them they touch the floor a little but it’s fine don’t plan on wearing flats with them anyway. Will buy again.

The jeans are really cute, but way too short for me. I am just above 5'8", and 145 lbs. I normally wear a size 6 at Express and size 28 waist in The Buckle brand jeans. I bought the size 8 in these jeans and the fit around the waist is perfect. I really like the hem and buttons, but the length hits above my ankle, which makes the wide leg look weird. Overall, not very figure flattering.

The jeans are very comfortable with just enough stretch. For reference I’m 5’3” and 130 lbs and got a size 8. I went back and bought them in black.
The jeans look great. They are baggy and oversized which is what I was going for. The quality is very good! For reference I am 5'7, 116lbs and got a small.

The jeans that I received don't look anything like the picture. They are supposed to be slim, but they are very baggy. The jeans are a solid color instead of the faded/distressed look shown. I'm 5'4 and they're a little long for me. They're also a size larger than expected. I am keeping them though, because they are very comfortable.

The jumpsuit is very cute but I felt like it was a little short. Material seems like it will shrink in one wash. I’m 5’5 and 120lbs I ordered a small, I’d recommend sizing up to avoid shrinking from wash!

The khaki jeans are super cute. I struggle to find straight/wider leg jeans without it being too baggy in the waist/hips and these look awesome! For reference, I am 5’6 115lbs and an XS fits well, if not a taddd bit snug.

The legs fit fine, isn’t super tight or super loose. More of a slim fit. They go right above my ankles which was expected. They are very tight around my stomach though and pretty high up few inches higher then my belly button. Not sure if I should size up Becuase I don’t want the rest of the pants bigger only the waist part not to be so tight. I got an XS. I’m 5’4” 95 lbs if that helps.

The legs of these pants are not as wide as other barrel jeans that I have, so are a bit more straight. This is a size medium and the waist is a bit tight, but good fitting everywhere else. Love the striped fabric, you don't see that a lot in denim. This could be dressed up or down with boots or heels. I'll probably hem these, as they are a bit long on me at 5'2". The front pockets are nice and deep, would fit even large phones, and the back pockets are large as well.
The length could be longer. I’m 5’8 and I ordered a medium. I would have given 5 stars if longer pant length. Good purchase for price.
The length is perfect, the fit is perfect. 5'3", 145 lbs, size M.

The length is perfect! I'm 6'1" 170lbs, I ordered these in my true size 8 long and they were PERFECT! I already ordered a second pair because I was so happy. The denim is firm but has enough stretch to be comfortable without losing shape. I've washed and dried these multiple times without any issues/shrinking. I got these on sale for around $16 so you definitely can't beat the value for the money! All around, I'm very pleased with these jeans.
The length of these jeans is perfect (I’m 5’8”) I didn’t like the belt but it is removable!

The leopard jeans are a nice variation to the common plain regular jeans. The leopard print is what I like the most about the jeans. The shape of the jeans is different from my usual skinny jeans and especially the legs and the hip feel a little boxy. I am 5'2" petite, 140lbs, 31" waist and 43" hips, and size 12 fits just right. If I gain a little bit more weight, I won't be able to fit. The jeans feel fine from my waist but there are some creases that form in the front, also the pants are too long but for somebody taller this won't be an issue. The fabric feels a little bit stiff and rough compared to regular jeans but after wearing them for a while they get softer and adapt better to the shape of the body. I wash the jeans in a cold regular cycle and dry them in the permanent press cycle in the dryer. I steam them to remove wrinkles. The pants didn't shrink and preserved the colors after washing.

The levi strauss signature was my favorite pre pregnant jean and when I saw these available for maternity I quickly ordered some. Im between a 12 and 14 in regular jeans. I have really wide hips 45 inches. I went with Large and they fit but I 5hibk a little tight plus I dont think they will last me long through pregnancy. The material is different then the normal levi strausd signature jeans, the material seem more thinner and stretchy more like jeggins than jeans, but still really nice style and quality I will be returning for a XL really hoping they will work for me. I got the XL and I love them, they fit great and I am confortable and feel more myself. I have been searching for some nice fitting and confortable maternity jeans and these are it! I will be ordering more colors.
The Levi’s low pro are not your low jeans! I ordered a size up to 31. I’m a usual 29, 5’5” 165. They are baggy and shrunk a tiny bit. I will reorder another wash in a 30.
The Levi’s low pro are not your low jeans! I ordered a size up to 31. I’m a usual 29, 5’5” 165. They are baggy and shrunk a tiny bit. I will reorder another wash in a 30.

The lighter green one is longer than the other style and runs a bit big in my opinion. I got the army green in the other style too and the medium in that style is either the same or almost smaller than the light green. But regardless very very cute and I love it. I'm 5ft, about 130lb so it's not mini mini skirt but it's mini lol

The Linen Capri Pants for Women Casual 2024 Summer Drawstring Elastic High Waist Capris Wide Leg Cropped Trousers are true linen pants. While they are thin they are not seethrough. They are lightweight, have an elastic high waist with a working drawstring, and are a great wardrobe addition for the summer months. I am 5'3" and very curvy, I ordered a size large to fit comfortably over my hips. These pants fit nicely, they offer plenty of room and comfort, the length hits about mid-calf and they have a nice flare. The loose flowy fit makes them perfect for hot summer days. The waistband is attractive and a shirt/blouse could be worn tucked or untucked. I look forward to wearing these capris throughout the summer and am very happy I made the purchase.

The main thing you should know is that I received this pair at no direct cost through the Vine program but just ordered and paid for a second pair because I liked them so much! The relaxed and stretchy fit is comfortable, and I like the look—a new one for me—plus my husband said they look cute—sold! The small is good for me; I’m just under 5’8” and about 120 lbs. I have a long waist so sometimes have trouble with the waist-to-crotch distance being too short; felt a little tight that way when I first put them on (the photos were taken then) but they relaxed quickly as I wore them. Update after some wear: I do agree with the other comment(s) about the scratchy threads. By no means a dealbreaker, and it doesn’t impact my enjoyment of these much, but there are some spots on the back pockets and belt line that catch your shirt or skin enough that I took off a star.

The material felt and looked like good quality, but there was a fabric defect right in the middle of the faded thigh area. (See photo). It's not obnoxious in the photo but it definitely stood out when I wore them! Also the inseam is barely 28", not the 29.5 as advertised (not for tall gals like me...I'm 5.9, 133 lbs before pregnancy.) Lastly, I'm only 11 weeks pregnant with twins but the band was already too tight. Will be returning Pronto!

The material has a really nice feel to it. Listed as 70% cotton/30% linen. They are flowy & full. Very comfortable. I am 5’4” & they are a bit long on me, but will be fine with a small heel. I mostly wear a size 12, occasionally a size 10 if an item runs large. I ordered an XL, but think a Large may of fit more appropriately, both in length & size. My suggestion would be to size down, if in between sizes like myself. They came in a vacuumed packed bag, so they were quite wrinkly, but steamed out nicely & easily. They are very pretty pants if a palazzo style appeals to you. The pockets are a decent size, but are not overly deep. I think they might look a little more swanky on someone taller than myself, but they are attractive pants. To me, the color is more of a periwinkle, than a true blue, but they are a pretty color. I wish there was a petite option, but these are stylish pants that can be dressed up or down. I would recommend, if fuller pants appeal to you.
The material is a bit too light to wear outdoor for me. But it's comfortable to wear indoor. For reference I'm 5'4", 115 lb and pictures showing me wearing size S.

The material is actually quality, not see through and the perfect color. Overall not bad BUT you will definitely have to iron these pants. You can’t get away with sticking them in the dryer for a few minutes to get rid of some wrinkles, and there will be a lot. I’m also 5’ 4” and they are the perfect length for me but if you were anybody else they would be really short

The material is durable, the pockets are long and wide. I love this skirt it is comfy and size L fits my 5”2 133lb pear shape build. Color gray/black denim is so cute. 10/10 recommend, will likely buy in more colors!
The material is good, the shorts are nice and comfortable. I'm very satisfied and bought other colors too. I’m 5’2” and weigh 157 lbs and bought a size L.
The material is good. Definitely long for a short person I’m 5’0 and had to fold the bottoms twice. True to size. Wide leg not straight. Would buy again for sure.

The material is great and the waistline fits really good, but the rest of the fit of the pants is awful. The legs are way to big and the pants themselves are way to long. I’m 5’5 so I could understand them being long if I was short, but I’m average height.

The material is pretty thin, actually see-through. They feel cheap and in person look cheap. Pictures look better than in person. Very sparkly, but the pants are not as long as the picture leads you to believe. I’m 5’4” and these pants hit me right at the ankle. I would prefer that they went all the way to the ground (like the picture). I thought about keeping them because the price is great but I am going to return. I ordered a size medium.

The material is soft and comfortable, I like the quality of the fabric. The fit is relaxed but not too baggy, so it has a good balance between comfort and style. The length is just right, falling slightly over my shoes, I’m 5’1 for reference. The sweatpants also have very deep pockets. Highly recommended!

The material is soft and stretchy, thicker than typical jeggings. They keep their shape thru several wears, no baginess at the knees. Cold wash, low heat dry with no issues. 5'4", size 6/8, wearing the 8. I would not consider these "wide leg", more like a straight boyfriend fit. I like the off-center buttons and zipper but wish it was more exaggerated. The strip of fabric for the topmost button is a bit thin. When there's a lot of movement or while pulling up the waist, my tummy peeks through. I'm now a true size 6 and love how they look. The fit is more baggy and slouchy and has a relaxed off-beat vibe. I would suggest ordering 1 size up.

The questions we all have were fully checked off. I’m 5’2, 194 pounds, and built like a box. Seriously, you could pack me in the same rectangle box these pants arrived in. I ordered the lighter beige (think French vanilla) and the darker khaki (not a light tan but a true khaki color). I was going for the quiet luxury or “old money” aesthetic that is timeless and put together. So, how do these pants fair against the luxurious box that is me? Pros: Tummy: I have one. Do these fit mom bellies? Yes. What about the pooch effect? A little. When I sit down there is a little pooching from the fabric but if I add a blazer or sweater then it’s hardly noticeable. There is zero, and I do mean zero, stretch in the waistband so if you’re between sizes I would go up a size. I am a new size 16, from an 18, and it took me a full minute to be okay with a fitted waist since most of my pants have elastic waists. Buttons and belts: The buttons (there are 2) were sewn on well. The zipper didn’t catch and after two flights of stairs to test out whether these pants could handle the demands of being part of my closet the zipper stayed put. Belt loops are wider so while a thin belt would still look cute I would go with a standard thickness in a belt to get that old money look. Length: I could walk barefoot in them as they’re wide legs and while maybe an inch too long they’ll do nicely with heels added to them. I walked outside with a pair of booties on and they did not drag the ground. The hem on the bottom of the pant legs is nicely constructed and gives a tailored look. Thighs: Did not make me look like a can of biscuits about to make an escape. They draped nicely over my luxury edition thighs and didn’t seem to hurt my feelings. Cons: There really aren’t any that would stop me from ordering more of these. They probably require hand washing, gentle detergent, or Woolite. (Photo attached for instructions). Can you see through them? I couldn’t. I could see the line but with shape wear that would solve the issue. Bumps and lumpy bits showing? A little but this is where shape wear comes in. All in all, these are high end quality with an everybody price tag. They’re pants with substance meaning they are true corduroy trousers with a little weight to them. They’ll likely do fine through autumn but I will add a second pair a size up so that I can wear footless tights under for an extra layer of warmth during winter. 10/10 do recommend!

The reviews on the sizing of these jeans helped me tremendously, so I figured I'd do my part and pass along some information. I've had my eyes on these jeans for about a year, and when I saw that the "antique wash" version was on sale for only $20, I knew it was time to pull the trigger and a new denim staple to my fall wardrobe. My only question was...what size? When I looked at the reviews, it was clear that I needed to study the sizing charts before making my decision. My measurements: height: 5' 7" waist: 26.5 in. hips: 38 in. thigh: a little less than 24 in. inseam: 32 in. On the Wrangler's website, the sizes on the sizing chart are all listed in even numbers while the sizes offered here on Amazon are in odd numbers, so that makes things confusing. On the Wrangler's website, my waist lines up with a size 0, my hips with a size 6 and my thighs with a size 14. According to Amazon, my waist lines up with a 0, my hips with a size 5 and my thighs with an 11. On the Amazon sizing chart, the very first column says "brand size". As far as I can tell, the first number represents the waist measurement and the second number represents the in-seam. These numbers are really and may be the most in determining what size to try. I knew the jeans were supposed to have kind of a baggy fit around the thighs, but I wanted them to be more fitted across the hips and backside area so they still gave off a feminine vibe. I decided to try size 5-32 first. The length was perfect. They fit well over my cowboy boots, rainboots and lace-up/hiking boots. However, the waist was gaping and the booty and thigh area were way too loose for my taste. I decided to send them back and order a 3-32 in their place. I am much, much happier with the 3-32's. The material is stiff and feels like traditional, high quality denim, but they are not too tight anywhere. They feel equally feminine and tom-boyish (which is my style, in a nutshell). I could wear these to do yard/barn work or I could dress them up a little with a girly top and different shoes and still feel cute. The $20 markdown was too good to pass up, and I'm really glad I took the time to find my size. On the Amazon size chart under "brand size", the 3-32's are listed as 27-32. Again, I have a 26.5in waist and a 32in inseam (basically 27-32), and that is the size that ended up working for me. The rest of the waist, hip and thigh measurements that correspond to the 27-32 row on the Amazon chart do not reflect my measurements at all, but had I based my purchase solely off the "brand size" column here on Amazon, I would have gotten the sizing right the first time. I hope this review helps somebody out there who's looking for a great pair of jeans. :) Sizes purchased: 3-32 and 5-32 (compared in the photos as "3" and "5", respectively) Size kept: 3-32

There are a lot of these same looking pants sold all over Amazon. I ordered some by the brand LUBERLIN and I definitely think the fit of these AMiERY ones are better. Be aware: The sizing on these pants is not traditional US sizing!! The 3XL runs waaaay small but not *AS* small as the LUBERLIN ones. For reference, I am 5'8 and typically wear a size 8/10 or M/L. Currently, at 8 months pregnant I wear size M in Motherhood Maternity jeans and in lounge wear I size up to a L. I decided to order the 3XL of these pants and they fit me comfortably over or under my belly. But they will definitely be way too big postpartum. I plan to order the 2XL for postpartum. If you are a true 3XL you will be disappointed by the fit of these. The material of the pants is very thin & light. The color Black #2 has a bit of "sheen" to it almost looking like a sateen, but it doesn't feel like sateen-it feels like jersey knit.

There's nothing more timeless than a good pair of basic blue jeans. The Dickies Women's Perfect Shape Denim Jean-Straight Stretch lives up to its name and offers tremendous value for well made durable pants that move with you while staying in place. These Dickies are the type of jean that you'll look forward to wearing if an effortless style and fit are what you're after. They fit loosely without slippage and flatter the form with its straightforward sleek design. My wife wears size 8-10 and the 10RG fits her 5'5 ½" frame just fine, albeit a little loosely around the crotch and derrière. That's fine with her given the comfort it provides and the classic style it affords. The stitching is solid and the quality button and zipper are signature Dickies. The material feels durable yet it's not too thick or bulky. The stretchy material infused in the cotton fabric perfectly accommodates an active lifestyle. The pockets are large and practical and can be used to store personal effects like wallet or phone. The length is versatile, working with both flats and heels. My wife loves boots, so these work well as a boot cut with it's stretchy design. But as the Florida weather warms up, sandals will be her go-to footwear and these look great with open-toe shoes. The Jean-Straight Stretch stretches the form and provides a slimming effect that works great if you choose to tuck in a shirt, but it's form fitting enough for untucked longer lengths as well. ~ Kort

I like the cotton fabric with a little stretch.

These 22Plus, Gloria Vanderbilt jeans have a huge amount of elastic give which does not allow it be body conforming in the places you want them to be. For example, they do not help you look shapely in the tush, hips and tummy. I am 5'8, 260 pounds with a generous tummy. The jeans do not hide much tummy nor subdue hips and are very loose in the bottom side of the rear, & legs due to the stretch. Without a body shaper on they are a tad loose everywhere. With a shaper on, they are tighter but still comfortable. Yet to me, still not the most flattering look. They fit a tad bit like mom jeans in my opinion. I almost exchanged them for a 20 Plus just to get a more flattering silhouette. But I decided against it as one thing that they definitely are is the most comfortable jeans I ever wore. So, I decided to keep them as I am of the age (56) that comfort trumps style. My first designer jeans at age 14/15 in 1979 were Gloria Vanderbilts (see me in them in 3rd pic) . So buying these were like visiting an old friend that you greatly missed. If nothing else, I am decidely loyal to old friends and our fond memories.

These are a fun, light weight goucho-style pant. I am in love with the cool toned 'blue grey' color, which is going to be my neutral alternative to olive bottoms that tend to be too warm-toned for my other favorite clothes/colors. I like the pockets, and the (matching!) zipper and button waist, with an elastic band at the back. There are belt loops all around the waist the lay very flat, so won't gap out be super obvious if you choose not to wear a belt. The stitching and seams are fine, but not super quality. The fabric is a (very wrinkly) linen style, that I found comfortable and not as itchy as another review had mentioned. I like the length on my 5'2" frame. I do think the size chart is a bit inaccurate in the US numerical listing but not necessarily in the measurements, which can get tricky; I followed my hip measurements so I didn't get too much fabric gathering which made the waist just a little tighter than I'd prefer, though the elastic in the rear waist helps. Overall I like these pants, but I think they might be priced a little high for their quality ($33.99 at time of review). I'm excited to see how much wear I can get out of them before they fall apart this spring/summer.

These are a nice light pair of summer weight denim pants. I think they're meant to be crops, but I am 5'3"and these came down to my ankles (which I prefer anyway). The material is generally quite soft but some of the seams are very rough, especially around the waist area. It's like they chose to burn the thread ends rather than tie them off. I would make sure to always tuck in my shirt with these or wear substantial undergarments that come up to the waist (at least) to protect your skin. The thread roughness aside, I like the stretch of these pants. I wish they were a bit more structured in the midsection. Since they are so very high waisted, one would hope they would provide some tummy control, but these just move and stretch with you, which makes them incredibly comfortable. The green is very light and makes for easy pairing with many colors of tops and shoes. I normally wear an 8 or 29 and these pants in a large fit me well.
