This is seriously the most amazing pair of jeans! To start, I live in the Northeast where winters are long and cold. i have owned a few different pair of flannel-lined jeans that I paid MUCH more for than the listed price of these jeans. It's early but my initial opinion is that I like these better. When I took them out of the package, I thought they looked small so I tried them on right away. I'm generally a size 12 with most of my weight around the middle. I ordered large and they fit perfectly. Instant love! I wore them all day. There's just enough stretch to them to accommodate the places where it's needed without being baggy in places where you don't. At 5'4", I'm on the shorter side and the length is perfect for me. Note that they may be a little short on a taller person. The lining is soft and warm but not overly heavy so that you can transition from outdoors to indoors without overheating. The zipper and the button are sturdy and well constructed. One of my other favorite qualities of these jeans is the boot cut. It's logical that if you are wearing flannel-lined pants that you would also be wearing a bootie/boot yet other high end brands of flannel lined jeans typically have a straight or tapered leg which prevents that. This is one of the BEST items that I have ordered online!

This is the first pair of jeggings I've purchased, and I LOVE them!!!!!! They look and feel like real denim jeans! I'm not a fan of 'skinny' jeans, but I decided to give them a go (because I can't find anything similar in boot cut or flare without a hefty price tag). I've seen pairs of jeggings at Walmart that looked.. well... not good lol. I've also seen other people wearing jeggings and you can totally tell that they are, in fact, jeggings. They look kinda cheap? But not these, they look amazing. I've never felt more confident in any other pair of pants (is it technically 'bottoms'?). I'm very happy with my purchase. I will give you a break down of my body shape and weight. Something that I absolutely hate!!! I have fluctuated up in down weight wise my whole life. I have some extra skin as a reminder. But, here goes... I am roughly 5'8" and weigh 240. I am sort of awkwardly proportioned. According to me lol. I carry most of my fat in my belly. I have a hard time finding real jeans that actually fit me properly, because my legs aren't that big. My natural waist is 44, my hips are 54, and my lower hip measurement is 34. So when I try on jeans in a size I can get up high enough with my tum tum, the legs always look baggy and weird. Anyway, I think it's important to remember that I could be standing next to someone that is the same height and weight, and we will not necessarily be the same size. My weight is in my belly, but some are fortunate enough to have there's evenly distributed. I ordered an XL, because I fit in to a XL in yoga pants, and leggings. They fit me perfectly. The length, feel, and fit was amazing. They aren't as thin as leggings, but they aren't as thick as some denim jeans. They structure to them, so they don't highlight every bulge. I hope this helps someone.

This pants are beautiful no lie might get two or three uses but it's worth it .I'm a size 12-14 in pants 200lb 5'3 and XL was great they actually stretch since I'm short they fit me kind of long . My thighs do rub so a few times the sequence was sticky together but it wasn't annoying.

This pants are good quality material. They are soft, breathable and true to size. The elastic in the back is very stretchy. I'm 5'2, 165lb usually wear a size 10 but I decided to go for a 12 because I like the loose fit and I was a little worried about the lenght. But definitely it could be a size 10 or 12 for me. The pleated on these pants makes them look more elegant. You can use them for the office or with sneakers for a more casual and relaxed look. Im so excited to wear them.
This pants are so comfy and stretchy! They are so cute and fit perfect! I got a small and I'm 5'1 and 155lbs for a size reference!

This pants are stretchy and comfortable. The elastic waist is also comfortable and not too tight. They look very fashionable. However, they're very see through and short. As cash be seen in the pictures, the little tag on the side is clearly visible. I'm 5'6" and they were just long enough to reach my ankles. I would have loved a longer fit.

This pants are well made. I washed them and hang them to dry. No need to iron at all. I'm normally between a size 18 and a size 20. I purchased the 18's and the fit perfectly fine. I would prefer they were a little longer , I'm 5'9" they touch the top of my shoes. Another good thing: no pilling at all...
This pants fit great and the quality of the fabric is superb. Will be keeping it! I am 5’2” and I weigh 138. I have an athletic build and I purchased the size 6.

This Pants is encouraging me to lose more weight because I really love it. 😅 ZERO stretch on hip and waist area so Adult Medium Size feels SMALL too tight. I am 5’3 140-145lb medium built usually wears medium. I will keep this pants and hoping to wear it this summer if ever I manage to lose more weight. Large I think will best fit my waist but it will give more baggy look at the bottom so I don’t like it too baggy.

This polyester feels like cotton linen! Perfect for summer, comfortable and breathable. I’m 5’2” and 150 lbs, and medium fit me very well. I love how flattering these pants are! Will be buying it in a few other colors.

This product was a great buy! These jeans are stretchy and comfy. They purposely run long so you can roll them up. I’m 5’5 and medium was perfect with 2 folds up. Perfect pairing with any shoes! I’ll def be purchasing more. The only downside was I purchased these for a NASCAR race and the patch work is NOT black white checker. The white is light blue so I was bummed about it because I paired the jeans with black white checkered purse strap from LVYOUME. All and all the color wasn’t a deal breaker because they were comfortable. Great product for the price!

This review applies to all three pairs of FTCayanz "100% Linen" Pants. I used the size chart and ordered 2X. I tried them on, but instead of being a little loose, they were tight around hips and thighs. I will have to return, and I suspect that incorrect measurements is the reason for "frequently returned item" warning by Amazon. Before receiving, I thought maybe it was because of the fabric. The tag says they're 100% linen, but they feel a little too "silky" to be 100% linen; I'd guess they might be a linen-rayon blend. I own other linen & linen-cotton blend & linen-rayon blend clothes & I also do yarn work, so I am familiar with fabric texture. Even so, they're quite soft & I had hoped they'd fit. They did not. Much too tight. Hips were supposed to be 52" and I have 50" hips, so there should have been plenty of room to spare, they shouldn't have been tight at all. The legs are "straight leg" from hip to hem, so not for those of us who have meaty thighs. Unsightly in being revealing of lumps and bumps-- one reason to wear linen is to help hide that! The pants are on the thinner side, which means some transparency. The other thing to know about these pants is they do not have real back pockets, it's fake, but does look nice. I can't speak to value for money or shrinkage, but I wager even hand washing & line drying there'd be shrinkage (especially if there is rayon in them). Use caution. I'm 5'2" 220+ pounds, evenly distributed & consistently wear 2X during my slow weight loss. These 2X fit more like XL. Unfortunately, you can't trust their measurement chart, so buy at your own risk if you're plus size. I'll be returning all 3 pairs. The striped pair almost fit, and the dark grey was just slightly looser than the linen color pair-- inconsistent measurements even though they're all labeled 2X.

This review is for the “ Women's Casual Parachute Pants Baggy Hiking Pants Drawstring Elastic Waist Track Y2K Trousers” I always check size charts for any bottoms I order. Most of the time the outseam number stays below 42”. I am 5’10” and it is difficult in this big country to find tall and affordable fashion bottoms. So I was thrilled to see the 43.11” listed for the XXL. They fit perfectly in the length. The hip portion is huge and it looks like you carry a bit extra weight there. But I don’t care or mind! They machine washed and hung dry without issues. The drawstring cords are really long, you can wear them pulled in or loose for a wide leg look ( then they are slightly too short for me). So I wear them pulled and will cut them shorter. The current price of $30 gets to $27 with the 10% coupon. A great deal!

This review is for the “ Womens High Waisted Jeans Comforts Straight Leg Loose Stretchy Lightweight Tummy Control Trendy Jeans for Women”. I am tall 5’10” with a long inseam and always have trouble finding pants to fit me. These showed a 42.5” total length and when I saw the photos showing the large bottom seam stitching I got them. They have almost 2.5” extra material in the big bottom seam. I let the seam out and stitched them to the longest possible length. And yay, I got tall jeans! These jeans are high waisted and have wide legs. They are stretchy in the right places and are very comfortable, I washed and dried them and they did very well. I am not a big fan of the “whiskers”, they are more pronounced than in the listing pictures. It does not feel like they any tummy control All in all these are good jeans. The competing market is huge and prices range from $15 to $1500. At $41 they are a decent buy if you want wide leg, high waist and stretch.

This review is for the GRAPENT Wide leg palazzo pants, xx-large, dark blue. I usually wear an XXL in pull on pants, 17/18 in jeans, and XL (XXL is usually a little too big) in leggings. Approximate measurements are 41” waist and 51” hips, 5’6” and a 30 inch inseam reaches just to the floor on me. These pants are a delight to wear. They are unbelievably soft, thin, stretchy, and flowy. The style is meant to be oversized so definitely don’t size up even if you want a loose fit. The size chart was based on body measurement and not the dimensions of the pants. It says that XX-Large, US size 20 - US 22, waist 36.5 - 38”, hips 47 - 48.5”. My waist and hips are bigger than that and the pants still are a flowy fit on me. The waist has a few inches of smocking and is very stretchy. The fabric has a good horizontal stretch to it. These pants drag on the ground for me. I wouldn’t expect this with a 29” inseam but I measured and they are indeed 29 (maybe 29.25) inches. However even with the waist an inch or two above my belly button (which is perfect for me) the crotch sits a little low. It looks and feels good but that’s why the leg length is too long for me. They look like the could be a straight wide leg but are actually about 16” at the thigh and 20” at the hem. The pockets are huge and stretchy and don’t lay completely flat across the front but rather droop out a little at the top. As I mentioned, these pants are delightfully comfortable. They are a little too oversized and long for me to want to wear as everyday “real” pants. Instead I’ll wear them primarily as loungewear that I’m OK with wearing out of the house for something like running an errand.

This review is for: Dokotoo Womens Summer Boho Business Casual Smocked High Waisted Cargo Long Pants with Pockets These pants can wrinkle easily but with a steam iron all wrinkles came out easily. The pants are lightweight but comfy - but the downside is that they don’t look as flattering for a short person or someone with shorter legs. I’m 5’4” 120lbs and the size Small ordered had plenty of room to spare.
This runs so big, color was off. I am 5’4 and 110#. It looks sloppy on.

This set is comfortable. Good material. I think it runs a little big. I’m 5’2 and weigh 130lbs. I do have big boobs (34DD) and big butt and still found this to be a little loose. Not a ton, but if I looked down I can see my bra. I would still purchase again even after knowing this. I just wish it was a tighter fit to show off more of my curves.

This set is made of nice quality material and is so soft! I don’t love the way the pants fit, they aren’t very flattering. The top is very cute. Of course the pants are quite short, but at 6’2”, I expect that 😂

This set is really really soft and cozy. I'm currently 25wks. I ordered a L. For reference, pre pregnancy I was an 8/10 medium 5'9 160lbs, large chested, and my inseam is 34". These pants are short on me. I would prefer them to be longer. I do not plan on wearing this outfit outside of the house. I like the waist band. It is not tight on my pregnant belly. I am worried that it will shrink a little when I wash it, making the inseam even shorter. The sweater fits nicely. I will probably wear it post pregnancy as a normal sweater. The sleeves are long enough, which is a plus for my longer arms. All in all, I really like the coziness of this outfit. Its just not the most flattering thing on my pregnant body. But for lounging around the house, this a must!
This set is SO comfy. It’s a must have! I’m 5’1 wearing a size small. I would say it fits TTS!

This set is so incredibly comfy! I’m 5 feet, ~165lbs and I struggle finding sets that aren’t suffocating and also not too long. I got a medium size set and will definitely be getting another set or 2!

This set is super comfy but much thinner than I expected. It’s like pajama material if that makes sense. I probably won’t wear this out of the house as I originally intended but I’m ok with that. I also thinks it runs a tad big, but again, since I’ll be using it simply for lounging, that’s fine. For reference: I’m 5’7, size 6 and bought a M in the longer length and still have extra length which isn’t always the case for me. I’d say if you’re 5’6 or below, to get the regular length.

This silver "sequin" belt has a gorgeous band. It's a nice thick quality leather covered in multi-shades of silver glitter (that doesn't shed), outlined in silver beading ( not sequins). I adore the belt band, just wish the buckle wasn't 3 " wide and covered in VERY large rhinestones that seem a lil cheesy for an older woman like me. I just wear my shirt untucked in the front to camoflage the buckle, and got LOTS of compliments from girlfriends wearing it as slightly more demure bling. this skirt got SO many compliments at the UTK game! 🧡🍊 i’m 5’10 and the length was perfect. wore the large (i’m a 10) buy it! you’ll love it if you’re a mid 40s girl trying to have a little fun lol

This skirt is medium weight, stretchy denim that hugs your curves. I bought size M, gray color. As you can see, it is a darker gray, and is maxi length on me. I am 5’3” 133#. Overall I like the look, but am deducting one star for the cheesy fade marks. The stretch denim makes the fit comfortable and forgiving, and I really appreciate the deep front pockets!
This skirt is so cute and fits amazing ! True to size ! Doesn’t ride up! I purchased a medium im 5’2 and weigh about 143 lbs. I’ll be buying more colors for sure !
This skirt is super comfortable and flattering. I 4’11 and it was too long. Highly recommend for all my short girlies.

This skirt is super cute and stretchy! Still would not recommend sizing down. I’m 5’7 150 and ordered a small and a medium. The medium fit perfectly while the small was comfy also, just too short for me. Get the skirt! Thank me later.

this suit pant fits my wife perfect, she bought size 0 , for reference : she is 5'2" and 105 pounds , the fabric is amazing and very fine quality

The pleats give just the right amount of tailoring to the finished style, and the pockets are deep and usable. These are a great choice for a versatile work to running around town look.

This vest is very good and seemed to be good quality, but I got a size M and couldn’t button the top button without it cutting off my boobs in a weird way. I’m 5’3” and about 150 lbs, somewhat broad shoulders and chest.

This was a big hit for my Rosie the riveter Halloween costume! It was super comfortable and fit great. I got lots of compliments on it. I’m 5”3 160 lbs and 34DD I needed an XL. The large would have fit but not over my bust. A few threads starting to frill after a few wears and only one wash.
This was a nice suit great price, lots of compliments. I ordered a XL I am about 230 5'10

This was a very comfortable denim dress that was not too stiff and not too flimsy. Overall, the quality was better than I expected. The buttons work perfectly, being snug enough to hold the denim together and yet easier enough to button and unbutton. I'm 5'8" and I thought the length was perfect. It wasn't too short or too long. I really liked the deep pockets that could easily hold a phone, keys, or small wallet. The pockets are somewhat hidden on the sides and designed in a manner that does not stand out. A quick look at the dress and you might miss them. The sleeves were long enough to fit my longer arms and the elastic wrists helped prevent them from being too long. Overall, the dress has a baggy feel and is free flowing. In some ways this can be flattering as it hides your stomach. Other times, the extra baggy look can make you look bigger. I'm not sure that I would call it flattering, but it's not bad. Overall, I did like this dress a lot. It strikes a lot of balance and I feel it can be versatile. I do recommend this dress for most anyone looking for a denim dress. I really like the way the collar dresses up the look, too.

This was an impulse buy. Have trouble finding jeans that are comfy when it comes to my tummy. I don't like tight, snug fits. These jeans are a great fit. I like the color and feel of the fabric. They may look a tad short but they're not. I am kinda tall though. 5' 8".

This was exactly as I had hoped it would fit after reading the reviews. I’m 5’2, 165 lbs. and ordered a size (L). The jacket fits comfortable, with a little stretch and able to button up if I wanted. The material is light, not too thick making it versatile in any season. So happy with this purchase.

This was never meant for the internet.Y'all... This is what I ordered, and this is what I got. Like, WTF??? My sister almost peed her pants laughing at me. This was one of those dresses that could have gone either way based on the pics, but I’m so glad I took a chance. It’s totally adorable!! This was such a comfortable tunic and I love it because it can be dressed up or down. The fit was loose around my waist but perfect around my bust as I prefer it to be snug but not be too tight (bust size 34 DD).

This was the perfect belt to use for Stagecoach. Super easy to put on and take off. I got the smallest size and for reference I’m a 24” waist, 107 lbs & 5’1”. Will probably buy in other colors as well!
This was the perfect pant for our galaxy girl! Size small for a 5’7” daughter.

This was the second time ordering these pants. I am 185, 4’11 and usually wear a 16. After reading the reviews I sized down to a 15, that was a mistake. However the company sent one short and the other one regular so I will be returning the other color in hopes I can reorder the 17 short. I was hoping it would be a little longer (I plan to wear it with a short dress). I’m 5’8”, 155lbs, broader shoulders, 36A, 30” waist, and 40” hips and got a size Medium.

This white denim jacket is very nice! The material is high quality as is the color. The buttons aren’t loose or cheap and all the pockets are real! I was hoping it would be a little longer (I plan to wear it with a short dress). I’m 5’8”, 155lbs, broader shoulders, 36A, 30” waist, and 40” hips and got a size Medium.

This wide jean is nice. I like the fit. I’m 5’3 and the length is perfect on me. I like that the jean material has a stretch to it and feels soft, it’s not stiff. It’s very comfortable to wear. Jean is high wasted. No odor. Fabric is not itchy. Great quality.

Though I really did like this outfit, I think I should've sized down one. It fit really cute but was big at the top. I got plenty of compliments on the way it fit but if it fit a little smaller at the top I could've worn it open instead of having it buttoned up. It was kinda long for me (I'm only 5 feet) but I washed it and got it to shrink just enough. Overall I loved it.

TLDR — Size down at least 1 size, if not 2. Length is good for some petites (5'1" to 5'3") and regular height (up to 5'7"). I'm 5'2" and weigh between 165-170 lbs. depending on the time of the month. My measurements are 41" Bust - 36" Waist - 41" Hips. I generally wear size 10-12 or L-XL depending on the brand, fabric, type of clothing, etc. I have a small FUPA but no butt, so occasionally I can size down to a size 6-8 or M (Medium) depending on the stretch of the fabric. I'm short-waisted and usually wear mid- or high-rise with a short or regular inseam depending on the pants and type of shoes I'm wearing. I ordered size L (Large) as suggested by Amazon in the color white, and it's too loose and baggy overall. The smocked waistband is comfortable and super stretchy. The white linen pants have lining inside all the way down except 6" from the bottom so it's not see through! You can't see the deep pockets... maybe very faintly if you're looking really hard in the picture. (I wish other white pants are lined like this, because there is nothing more annoying than having white pants that are practically transparent where you can see the pockets or even your nude underwear.) Because the pants are lined, it does add some noticeable weight to the overall feel of the pants, and along with the super stretchy smocked waistband, it causes them to slowly slide down. The pockets are deep and a great addition, and you could fit a phone in there, but because of the overall "heaviness" of the fabric and lining, the phone would definitely pull down the pants because the waistband won't support it. For this reason, I would size down to a size Medium for a relaxed fit or even a Small for a tighter fit. I'm only 5'2" and have the smocked waistband pulled above my belly button. The length of these pants are 41.5" with an inseam of 27.5". I'm wearing 2" platform sandals where the pants are almost grazing the floor. If you're shorter than 5'2" and don't want to wear 3" heels/ wedges/ platform sandals, these may be too long for you. Check the size chart for the overall length of the pants. According to the tag, this is made in China and it's only 30% linen and 70% rayon. It says, "Dry clean only. Do not bleach. Steam iron." I used a garment steamer to get the wrinkles out. The fabric isn't scratchy like typical linen since this isn't 100% linen. It felt light and breathable, even in hot, humid 92°F weather. Sitting on them obviously causes them to wrinkle in the back but the front remains mostly wrinkle free. Based on the size chart, I think going by the hip measurement would be best. Size medium for me would provide a much better fit where the waistband is hopefully a little tighter but the pants are still relaxed enough, while size small would provide an overall tighter fit. I'm debating on washing these to shrink them down a little as others have mentioned. Aside from the dry cleaning care instructions, who has time for that?! I do like these pants a lot and will order another pair or two in different colors and smaller sizes. I'll update my review later if I remember.

To begin with my measurements: hips 47 inches and waist is 37.5 inches. 5'5 feet tall at a whopping 190 lbs. I am boldly sharing my photos of my muffin top with you all. I hope the visual helps xD I ordered size 16 with inseam 30 (no option for short, regular, or long, but would have bought short if prompted). So these clearly ended up far too small. Getting them on was a battle. I had to do a combination of jumping and wiggling to pull them up. Even harder getting them off. I've got size 12 short Maurices jeans that fit better than these (although those are too small as well). Last year I bought size 14 short pull on jeans from Belks that are now too small. I thought maybe I am a size 16 now since I gained weight. I was so hopeful for these. My fiancée doesn't think they're worth returning, since I'm on a weight loss journey, but I wanted jeans that fit now rather than later. I wish I knew what size I need to order, sizing chart appears to be no help. I will say though, the material is very soft and stretchy (I did still get them on haha). I think these could be great with the proper size. I'm not sure in my case if one size higher is best or even two sizes higher.

I wish I knew what size I need to order, sizing chart appears to be no help. I will say though, the material is very soft and stretchy (I did still get them on haha). I think these could be great with the proper size. I'm not sure in my case if one size higher is best or even two sizes higher.

To begin, I love the length. I'm 5 foot 4 1/4 inches I weigh about 123 and it's long enough to cover my ankles, but it doesn't drag on the floor like some skirts or pants if I wear shoes with just 1/2" or 1" heels. I got the bright red and looks like good quality. Also, this is very comfortable and the slits are not too high - maybe about knee length? UPDATE: I now have this is four colors - white, red, navy and black; moreover I have ordered the gray striped pants as well. ANOTHER UPDATE: I now have another three colors, but here is something I noticed. Some waistbands are more relaxed than others; if you look it it, some have three seams - with one going through the middle of the waistband, which makes it a little tighter. Others have just two seams - the top and bottom, making them a bit more relaxed in the waist.

to start, I’m 5’5”, 120lbs, 34-25-38. These jeans fit like a glove honestly. I saw a lot of reviews talking about the material seeming “cheap” and how these AA jeans aren’t like the ones they’ve made in the past. Maybe I was just lucky, but the jeans I got are SUPER thick and heavy. There is NO stretch, so you’ll have to break them in by just wearing them for a few hours. I’m small but pretty curvy and I love the way these jeans fit on me! They compliment my body type just right. Only issues I have are the fact that they have no stretch which makes it super uncomfortable to sit down. I would strongly advise you to size up by 1. When I originally bought a pair back in 2013, I could fit a 24 easily. This time around I decided to go with a 26 knowing my waist size would probably fit a 25. I’m so glad I followed that advice! while the jeans are a little too big in the waist(pic #3), it was nothing a belt couldn’t fix as you can see in the photos, and they flatter me very well. Had I gone smaller I wouldn’t have been able to get them over my hips, they are already snug as is. overall, I’d highly recommend these jeans. They gave me the perfect “mom jean” fit while still accentuating my curves. I’m so happy I took a chance and purchased anyway despite the reviews.

Tons of stretch! Im 187lbs, and I’m wearing a large in the picture. Wasn’t too short in the back,so it’s definitely “big butt friendly” and you can pull and tie the front for the desired “skort” look.
Too long for me :( I’m 5’4, 117lb(36B, 26,35) it fitted kind of loose as well.
Too long. They fit great in thighs and waist though. Wish they were shorter by at least two inches or more. Love the feel of them. I got the Large. I'm a muscular 180lbs and 5'4".
Too long. They fit great in thighs and waist though. Wish they were shorter by at least two inches or more. Love the feel of them. I got the Large. I'm a muscular 180lbs and 5'4".

Took a couple of returns to find the right size as the different colors/materials seem to fit inconsistently but it’s 100% worth the effort! These are so comfortable, stretchy and flattering - I was literally dancing around in them because I’m so tired of living in leggings. For reference I’m 5’7”, typically a size 2 or 4 depending on the brand. The smalls of the blue jeans were too small, these pictured are a medium. However I also ordered the black in medium but they’re so big they ripple on my legs, so I need a small. Super inconsistent, the trial and error is worth it though!

Top 3 issues : 1) The band at the top looks wide but the actual compression fit is a thin band inside of it at the top so the fit is awkward and makes a belly pooch shape. 2) shows any tiny bit of cellulite you may have. I’m 5foot and 118lbs. I do have some cellulite on lower buttocks and upper thigh but it’s not a lot. This made it look worse and was rather depressing. I even tried a size up and other color options. 3) you can see the outlines of the inside pocket area. Not smoothing like I hoped.

Totes recommend for anyone who loves overalls. I was happily surprised how comfy these wear since they have stretch and honestly now they’re my most comfortable pair of overall I own! I got the medium (could have gotten small but I prefer things a little bit loose especially overalls). I’m about 5’2 and 140 pounds for reference. Such cute pair, recommend for sure! Treat yo self!!! ✨✨✨

Traveling internationally this weekend and doing my last minute Amazon shopping and came across this gem. I am 5”5 and weigh 190lbs and ordered a LARGE (which is my normal size) and is true to size. Waistband is comfortable and has a generous stretch to them. Super flowy and not see through at all. The inside lining reminds me of the structure of black our curtains (if that makes sense). Anyways — get these, you won’t be disappointed!

Tried these on and yes, they are very comfortable and they stretch which is great. However, they were too loose in the pocket and waist area. I'm 5'7, 134 lbs and I ordered a size 6, but they were too big. I do like the color and wash I will most likely reo rder down to a size 4.

True high waisted shorts. When I first tried these on I wasn't sure if I liked them or not as I'm still getting used to high waisted jeans/ shorts, I'm more used to a mid rise simply because I have a short waist. The fringe/ Frey was pretty long so I did trim off about half which in my opinion made it look better. After that I liked these shorts better. I'm 5'0", 110lbs and I got the small and they fit perfect. There is some stretch to them and I like how the side is cut so they legs are not tight around my thighs. Cute shorts for a casual summer look, just have to get used to the high waist, other than that they're great.

true to size, good fit for me that like a little support in the abdomen, at first they were a little tight but they stretch enough after a while, perfect length (I'm 5'6) they are very nice and you feel and see the good quality. Definitely buying again another color and maybe try a size up too and see which one fits better, but loved this pair of jeans
True to size, I ordered a M, 145 lbs and 5’7. Stretchy and the perfect length!
True to size. I’m 5’2 and this is a size medium.

Truly loved this dress but as you can see the chest button was just too loose so it had this big gap, it kept coming undone during the day also. I may have someone try sewing it for me but otherwise it’ll end up just hanging in my closet never to be worn again, depressing af. I’m 5’6ish, 150lbs ish and the length if anything was too long, but likely couldn’t have sized down because of the buttons. Those damn buttons.

Trust me when I say I love these slacks. I do, I really do. BUT they aren’t true black. They’re more of a black blue. Unfortunately, Lee does not have a true black in this type of pant so I’ll sadly be returning. The length, fit, style is absolutely perfect but it needs to match this blazer. Perfect length and cut for heels. I’m 5’4, 175lbs, and a typical size large or size 10/12 in pants. I got a size 10 and they aren’t too tight, too loose, they’re just right. Docking a star for the color.

Trying to find jeans that fit is hard enough at stores and boutiques. Finding jeans online that actually fit and flatter!! Who knew it existed! I am 5'7", 132 lbs, and have a slender/thin body type. I am also a mom of four, and don't have abs. I typically wear a 27 inch waist pants, and I ordered these in Size Small using the Size chart posted with the item description. I am shocked I got a quality Jean pants that fit like a glove! I will comment that they don't hug the butt all the way around the bottom, but that is because they are flared capri loose styled. These jeans are so cute. If you are able to find both the style and fit you love, I suggest you buy them in all the colors, they are just so nice. The jean has a little bit of stretch in them, and the pant leg has a raw hem so after a few wears and washes, I anticipate some fraying which is okay! They fit, they flatter, they are nice quality!

TTS fit. 5'4" 118# and purchased size S. Fit is great, they do not slide down, they stay put. The inside fleece is nice. Was in 40 degree weather and they worked. Not sure they would work for anything too much colder for warmth. But, could possibly be used as under ski pants' protection. Color was just as expected. A very nice wine / cranberry color. Would purchase again.

Ughhh I really wish it were shorter, I really loved it I was so sad....The shape and especially the Softness of the fabric Wow!!!! Im 5'4.5,130lbs got the Medium, fit beautifully but I just didn't like how it went all the way to 'my thighs...such a pity!!! If you are looking for this length or are 5'9 or taller then I'd highly recommend getting it!

Underneath I wear Amazon's "high waisted capri leggings" to give them "structure" and makes them go from looking like a flimsy pair of $20 polyester pants to $150 poly-blend SLACKS with a lining. FYI: I am 59 years old wear a medium and am 5’8 @ 160lbs

Unfornatly, (or fortunatly for you) I did not take photos of this product on. It was atrocious. I have never put anything more unflattering on my body. I wanted to be one of those cute WFH girls but this loungewear was not made for me. THIS IS NOT FOR US SHORT GIRLIES. I am 5'3" and have more of a rounded, curvy body shape. This set is made for tall, thin girls. The legs were too long, the shirt too baggy. The pant hugged all the wrong curves. The fabric made it so it stuck to all the wrong parts of my body and sucked me in. I looked like Patrick the Star in the pink one. Horrible. I also do not feel the quality justifies the price at all. Pros - Came as pictured Cons - Not for short or curvy girls - Fabric sticks to body and accentuates EVERYTHING - Quality not that great to justify the price - Length too long for short girls.

Unfortunately I didn't take a picture of the packaging, however, they come rolled in a plastic bag. The problem is that they were "moist" and therefore had a smell which is why I removed a star.For reference, I am 5'3, wear a very loose size 12, tight size 10 (US sizes). I ordered a medium which according to the size chart, this should be for US sizes 8-10. I ordered a Medium as an incentive to push me to lose weight/fit into these pants by the end of the year. As you can see by the picture, they fit now - love it!! They are NOT tight, they are loose, comfortable and can bend with ease. They are VERY stretchy and while I don't necessarily like how they look on me yet, BUT you can see that they are very well made.The stitching is great, the pockets are very deep. I put my iPhone16 (for reference) in the front pocket and bent forward and it didn't come out. I place it in the back pocket and it moved up a bit but 90% of my phone was still in the pocket so it did not fall out which is always a plus! The pants are a bit long but I am short (again, I am 5'3) so short heels/boots will fix the issue. The pockets don't "bulge open" because of the material and they are really soft and comfortable!Highly recommend but wash these first - no, they didn't shrink.

Unfortunately I ordered the wrong size. Should have gotten an x-small as these are a tad bit big on me - a little more oversized than I like. For reference, I am 5ft 2 in, 120-125 lbs, athletic build, and size small/x-small fits me best. All the stitching and sewing is done well with no open seams, overlapping of material or loose hanging thread. The Jean material is lightweight and has no stretch. These are fun jeans!

Unfortunately these are being returned. The color is perfect but the fit just did not work. I ordered an XL and they made me look much larger than I am. I think a Large would have been too short but I'm not sure on that as we didn't order the large. Color - 5 stars Waste - 5 stars super stretchy to fit comfortably Length - good but the crotch is low to make it long enough for a person 5 ft 8 inch Material - obviously by the pic you can see it will wrinkle horribly but it is soft Other - Weird pouch in the front where it bunches A Slender person could definitely pull this off but someone in a Large / X Large may not like the fit.

Unfortunately these pants are poorly made. I believe they came with a hole which I did not noticed upon arrived due to the loose and relaxed fit. The first time I wore them was to a professional work conference in which I randomly looked down and notice a large tear in them. As a thin, 100 lb, size 0 wearing women I’ve never had clothing rip on my body. I was sitting in various lectures all day and was not doing any unnatural positions to greatly stretch/strain the material. Cute but danger as they made begin to randomly unravel on your body during wear

Unfortunately this jacket is too big for me; I'd recommend sizing down. I ordered an XS in rinse wash (dark navy denim) and it was too big. For reference, I'm 5'5 with a long torso, 34A, and 115lbs. The jacket itself, as well as the sleeves, seemed oversized. The material is stiff and heavy, which I feel is a matter of preference with jean jackets... It doesn't bother me. The button closures are a bit tight and cumbersome to use, but would probably loosen up over time. The light stitching provides a nice contrast.

Unfortunately, I really do need every bit of the 32 inch inseam that was advertised. I will be sending these back as they do it just a little bit too short for flats. Would be ok with tennis shoes but that’s not why I needed them. I also feel like the hemming at the bottom was shorter than shown but that’s just my impression. They are very stretchy and would be comfortable to wear, just not long enough for me.

Unfortunately, I'm disappointed in the pants; the quality is very good, but sizing (for small people) is way off and far too big! I am petit/ athletic (5'2/ 105lbs), I normally wear a XS/2 so I ordered a 2. As you can see the pants were swimming on me! They don't make these pants in a 0, but considering how big the size 2 was I wouldn't bother trying the 0. Sadly, sizing in the US is totally askew! There is absolutely no way I am a size 0 in other countries, but here, goodness knows what size I am. Sadly, this seems to be a thing here in the US, sizing is getting bigger and bigger! And extra extra small people are sent packing to the kids department. Where can us smaller ladies buy a pair of pants that are true to size!!!

Unheard of, jeans that fit the first try! Most of my jeans are size 6, I figured M 8/10 would be a safe bet knowing these jeans are 71% cotton, another plus. I had my doubts when I saw the tube shaped package they arrived in. I tried them on pre and post laundering. Washed in cold and hung to dry, as per directions. I’m too nervous to put them in the dryer + the tag states not to. No shrinkage to . I’m 5’7”, 138 and long waisted, so these do not fit very high rise on me, which I’m fine with. The denim has a nice give and stretch - thankfully. I am not a fan of jeans that aren’t a little stretchy. Had a hard time choosing the color, this is ‘darkness blue’, it offers some leeway if they fade over time. Plus, I feel the darker colors can be more flattering as well. I think these are a nicely styled, quality pant and are on trend. Easy to wear year round with a variety of tops and shoes. Four usable pockets and belt loops. Very pleased with all aspects of these new jeans!
Unless you’re over 5’5” or want to roll them up. I am 5’2” 170lbs ordered XL and too big and too long like extra long. The legs Were loose and straight not skinny at all.

Until today, I have never selected the "buy again" prompt on Amazon, but these pants are ridiculously cute and comfy; I need to and will own more. I'm 6'0 and 185 lbs and they are perfect. I had a Zoom meeting earlier in the day and wore a solid black work top. For evening, I changed into a Slayer t-shirt, but kept the pearls on. They are super easy to dress up or down. Get you some!

UPDATE 2: I have been wearing and washing these at least 1-2x a week and they’ve held up great so I purchased another pair and it will be delivered today. 🥰 I was soooo worried a L/XL wouldn’t fit me because I’ve never had luck with apparel that’s meant to fit multiple sizes, but these are wonderful!! So glad I took the chance! I am somewhere between a 14 and a 16 depending on style and brand. These are buttery soft as claimed and very stretchy. I had my husband check if they are see through by doing squats and bends in the sun and he says no. They fit great in every way, including length. I have a 32” inseam and they hit right at my ankle. I do have a very short torso and the waist band sits a few inches above my bellybutton almost to my chest but that is the same with every high rise anything ever. 🙃 The stomach band is wide but I wouldn’t consider these tummy controlling at all, but the thick/wide band does keep them in place. They are fantastic and I will be purchasing an additional pair. I love them so much I wanted to post my review right away but I will come back with an update after they’ve been washed and dried. UPDATE: Washed these leggings on a regular cycle with my other clothes and dried them normally. They are in the same condition and size as they were when they went in. So glad they didn’t shrink at all and the rips are not stretched, distorted, or changed in anyway.

UPDATE 2: third year in a row buying these shorts for summer they are my favorite! I wanted to give an update on sizing because I’ve been working out and lost weight. I am now 5’4” 121lbs and size 2 fits perfectly! Although my shorts from the last 2 years are still in great shape they don’t fit anymore. These shorts last for a long time. :) I am buying 8 new pairs for this summer in size 2 because they are literally all I wear lol! I used to hate shorts but with these they cover my cellulite and give me confidence! No more long pants in the summer. Yay! I also started to order my step daughter these because her school dress code requires girls to wear longer shorts and she likes them as well. She’s 5’3” 135-140 with a straight figure and she likes the way size 4 fits. UPDATE 1: I ordered these last summer I think? I still am loving these shorts! I am back to order more for upcoming warm weather here in Texas. Black is finally back in stock- yay! I ordered a black pair and love them. They fit the same as the dark wash jean ones I have. I actually think I like the black ones even more than the dark denim, I didn’t think that was possible! Needless to say I definitely recommend these. I’m ordering now because when warm weather comes these sell out like hot cakes! Get yours now ladies! They have become mostly the only shorts I wear besides Nike athletic shorts for working out. I need to order multiples because I wear them constantly. Not having to pull your shorts down from riding up between your legs all day is amazing. Curvy ladies will know what I mean! I’ve tried so many other shorts and none of them compare to these. The only Bermuda shorts I’ve ever found that are flattering on me and don’t make my short legs look ridiculously frumpy! I wish they had even more washes for me to order because white and light wash aren’t my thing, not flattering on my big thighs. I only wear medium, dark or black wash denim. Although I’m tempted to go outside my comfort zone and possibly try the light wash.... ****Original review: I’m 5’4” 135ish and I ordered size 6. I range between a size 4-6 or 27-28. I’m normally a 4 in jeans. Size 6 is comfortable, ever so slightly loose. The size 4 fits as well (I tried both) but I just preferred the size 6 because it had a little more wiggle room. The pictures of me posted in the shorts are of the size 6. A lot of the reviews said to go up a size and I can see why because the size 4 was pretty fitted on me. I don’t like my shorts very tight fitting. Unless you are not curvy or very slim, then you may not need to go with your larger size. Or maybe you want a super tight fitting look, in that case you could probably stick with your normal size. They are extremely comfortable and flattering especially in the dark wash. I am a 30 year old toddler mom and these are very cute and practical for me! I don’t like short shorts so these are a win win. I don’t have to worry about them riding up and pulling them down during the day. Ladies who have thick thighs that rub, a big bottom and/or mommy tummy these are for you! I’m so glad to have found some comfy and cute longer length shorts that look flattering. They have a nice stretch and they don’t give me muffin top or cut into my stomach. I think curvy and/or plus size girls will love these too! I ordered like 5 pairs because I love them so much lol! The only slight issue I had was they stretched out a little bit when I wore them, but it wasn’t a big issue. They weren’t totally bagging out or anything. The only other thing would be I wish the back pockets were blank with no wavy line, not a big deal, just personal preference. Overall they just fit my daily casual mom style. I like how the hem is finished with a little roll, makes them look more pulled together in my opinion. They look cute with tanks, tees or even long sleeves! I like how they have a skinny leg, otherwise Bermuda shorts tend to look frumpy on me if they aren’t fitted to the leg. Levi’s always comes through for me. These definitely look cute and classy and more expensive then the price tag! If you hate short shorts you need these! They are a total confidence booster for those of us who want to hide cellulite, big thighs or just prefer a little more coverage. No inner thighs sticking together in the heat with the longer length lol! Lastly, you can’t beat the price!!

Update in September 2024: Well, I did buy them in two more colours. And I'm not particularly glad I did. The worst were the Ecru ones. A total disaster. Otherwise, I wouldn't have knocked off a star, but if it weren't for the blue ones, I might have knocked off two! I think my ecru pair is defective. All three pairs I ordered were 12 Long. In the first two new pictures, I aligned the crotch of my original blue pair with the ecru. The latter are lower in the waist (not a plus, for me) and so much shorter in the legs I would think they weren't "Long", although it is written on the labels. They barely touch the top of my foot, which definitely doesn't work with this cut! There is a lot more stretch in the ecru ones, which makes them sort of clingy. On the positive side, they are not see-through, in the sense that they are opaque. But you can see the outline of every bump and dimple on my knees. and the fabric clings so much that the ankles flare out, although when I laid them on the other jeans, I could see they are also cut that way. The blue ones have a slight taper, which makes them look straight on the body, since most people have larger calves than ankles. The ecru ones actually flare out a tiny bit at the very bottom, and it looks terrible on (you'll have to trust me, I have not taken a photo and have already gotten rid of the jeans!). Sadly for me, I live overseas and sending the jeans back would probably cost as mush as the price of the jeans, so I have given them to a much shorter woman in hopes she can cut them off above the weird little flare and get some wear out of them. I also think one leg is longer than the other. It looked like it on, and it looked ike it when I laid them on the bed. They were definitely new and had all the tags on, but there was a small brown spot on the back on one leg. I think it will wash out, but I didn't bother with it because the jeans were so hopeless. I think it might be fly p**. I also bought the Washed Black. I have kept them and I think I will wear them. Thankfully, since they cost $48 US, which is quite a bit for an ordinary pair of jeans. They are also smaller than my blue pair, but I can wear them. They are longer than the ecru pair, a smidge shorter than the blue. The colour is nice and they fit better than Ecru (I forgot to mention that the ecru pair comes up higher in the front than the back!!!). As they should be, they are higher in the back than the front, where they are about two inches below my belly button (of course, I am tall, but then again, these are supposed to be long). They are also stretchier, more synthetic than the blue ones, but less than the ecru ones (synthetic jeans are not a good thing in my book). But they are okay. I think Anchor two years ago rates a 4.5, Washed Black in 2024 rates a 3.5 or maybe 4, and the Ecru ones I received on the same day rate a solid 2!!!!!!!!!!! On the plus side: These are my jeans. They fit very well and are very comfortable. Most of the time, Lee jeans in size 12 Long are as good as it gets on me. I need a 34" inseam and these, hmm, they might be 33", but they are okay. (I'm 5'11") Although I prefer a high rise, and these are mid rise, they are okay and do not give me a muffin top. They are straight leg, but pretty narrow. Just that little bit of extra fabric at the ankles so you can tell they're not jeggings. Actually, they're nothing like jeggings, because the fabric is fairly thick, although nice and soft and doesn't stifle you like plastic (it's 30 degrees Celsius/86 fahrenheit where I am right now, so that's important). The colour is Anchor and pulling it out of the package, I thought it was darker than expected. Then I realized they have those fake wear marks down the front. The faded part goes to below the knee, so that it looks like I have really long thighs and short calves, whereas I am the exact opposite. But it's not a deal breaker. And there is a little whiskering on top, although not too much. I would prefer an even colour all over (hey, I'm on the far side of 55) but it isn't too bad. I really like these quite a bit, so I am going to go back and look at other colours. Hope this helps!

Update: 10/11/23 OK, what I thought last time is a defect is actually how these pants are made- so weird. But I like the way they fit so I’m keeping them this time and I wore them to work today.🤷‍♀️ Original review: I’m so sad!! The pants fit perfectly, they look super cute on my butt, nice fabric/color, perfect length for my 5’6” frame. Size 10 does stretch very well so fits me at 168 pounds. But the darn things arrived today with two WONKY inseams with weird pleasing on the inseams where it doesn’t belong. I can’t wear them because they look stupid. So back they go and hopefully the replacement pair will have normal inseams. My fingers are very crossed!! I will update my review if the next pair arrives and are not defective.

UPDATE: 21 weeks & S still fits great. Belly panel slouches & has a run in it which is unfortunate. Still love them in every other way. Perfect maternity jeans! I’m 5’6” 125lbs pre pregnancy & usually wear a 2/4. I’m 16 weeks along & 135lbs right now & a small fits like a glove. I may order another pair in a medium for later in pregnancy if these get too tight. They are pretty stretchy so I may be able to keep wearing them for a while if not the whole time. Length is perfect & calves are not too tight to make it hard to get them on & off. I like that the top panel has a little room left for growth & sits high so I don’t have a line across the middle of my belly when I wear them.

UPDATE: 6/7 - I just received my order for the second pair I bought. Since I loved the first ones ( see original review below) I decided to buy more. I have another pair on the way and I hope they fit like the first. These that I got today are black with blue specks all over them and peachy pink and white flowers all over. Not the cutest but as comfortable as I thought they’d be, I don’t really care too much. Well…..these things were tight! Like I’ll have ZERO room as my belly (and probably hips too) begin to grow more. I’m disappointed. I’m not even going to keep them because they won’t last me even month I’m sure. I have big legs, big ankles and I just don’t always feel confident enough to wear leggings. Some days I want to throw some baggy pants on other than yoga or sweat pants. They’re a little long but I don’t care too much. They’re thin and the waist doesn’t dig into my stomach at all. They’re pretty loose and airy and I feel like they’ll be great in the heat. They’re not stretchy but I still have room to grow. I’m also looking for something with busy prints so I can let my jiggle, jiggle, and not wear tights and all that. I’ll grab a few more pairs. Oh…I got a 3X and they’re still loose but I’m Ok with that. I’m about 230 lb, 5’3”.

They’re not stretchy but I still have room to grow. I’m also looking for something with busy prints so I can let my jiggle, jiggle, and not wear tights and all that. I’ll grab a few more pairs. Oh…I got a 3X and they’re still loose but I’m Ok with that. I’m about 230 lb, 5’3”.

Update: Disappointed in these. After 2 wears and washes the stitching is coming undone on the back pockets. They might be the perfect legging. I love the back pockets for my phone. They are a nice material that doesn't show your underwear lines. The length is perfect and I am 5'11. Went back immediately to order the other colors.

Update: EVERY single time I wear these pants, I get compliments on them! I went and bought them in other colors! - Originally I ordered these because I needed bell bottom pants for a 70’s themed party. I love them so much and plan to wear them regularly. I love the high waist and the fabric belt they come with and they are surprisingly comfortable. I ordered two sizes since I wasn’t sure how they would fit but I think the sizing chart provided is accurate. I kept the size 4, I’m 5’2” and fluctuate between 125 -130 lbs. They are just a tiny bit long for me personally and I think the fabric will wear at the heel with regular wear unless I were to wear a shoe with a little height.

Update: Got the right size (25), attached a new image of the fit above. Original review: I couldn’t decide between getting a size 27 or 28, and Amazon’s recommended size was 28. I got the 27 because it would arrive in time for my outing, and it still ended up being way too big (I prefer skinny/bootcut jeans to fit tightly), so the Amazon recc would’ve been way off. The Amazon size recc is based on just your height and weight, not all your measurements, and it messed up another order of Levi’s jeans I got, so I would trust the size guide instead! Other than that, the jeans are amazing. They’re comfortable levels of stretch while still being thick and giving quality, the deep blue color in combination with the contrasting thread in the “Casting Shadows” color is just 🧑‍🍳💋 chefs kiss perfect. Also, in the last image you can see it has the Levis stamp and tag so it’s definitely official, at least my variant! It also comes from the official Levis store and Amazon is strict against impersonators so I don’t think this is fake like that one reviewer claimed. Some Levi’s jeans have a tag that’s just red, without the logo, I own some of those. So yes! Buy it, but make sure to order the right size! My measurements: Height: 5’7 Weight: 120lbs Natural waist: 27 in Belly button: 29 in Hip: 33 in Butt: 37 in

Update: I loved my first pair from 2020. I came back to order another pair in the same color and same size. The new pair in 2024 is awful. The fabric is much thicker, stiffer, less stretch, higher rise, and just poor quality in general. I am so disappointed. Size reference: 32-24-35, 5’3, I am wearing size 2 regular in Cape Town in the photo. True size 2, comfy, and great style! I love it and ordered another pair in different color. Unfortunately the second pair (Blue Laguna) looked different with cheap white fabric label inside and a brown label in the back. The first pair (Cape Town) does not have any label inside and has a gold label in the back. But I ended up keeping both because they fit well.

Update: I originally wrote my review saying I didn't like how these look but I have changed my mind! I have them on with a cute summery top and some sandals and I have to say I like the way they look! Cute, casual, something I'd wear to coffee or for errands--which, I am about to run errands now and feel pulled together but effortless, because it was effortless. So glad I sized up because the waist is nice and loose in case I eat something makes me feel really full :D ha. In the photo I took to show you how it looks, it doesn't really show it as flattering as these are when you pair them with the right top and shoes--and again, that's when I want a comfy and casual look for errands or coffee, maybe lunch. ------- I usually wear a size 4 but I sized up to a 6 since I wanted a more looser comfortable fit for around the house and maybe errands or possibly coffee out with a friend. I hope the pics will help you to have an idea of fit and style of these jeans. I will note here that I don't find them to be 'stretchy' jeans but likely they will loosen up even more after wearing a while. Fit: For reference, I am 5'2" about 116-117 - my body type is 'curvy/hour-glass' but fit - I'm often called 'a tiny woman' though I see myself as curvy and powerful. Take in to consideration your body type. I have a small waist but if you have a different body type where your waist isn't small in relation to your hips, these may feel constricting at your waist. They come to just right under my belly button, where I like high-waisted pants as I don't like super high waisted. I am glad I did size up since while the legs are wide leg, the waist is 'nipped in' quite a bit so that it fits tighter--the size 6 allows it to be looser and comfortable around my waist area, while the size 4 would have felt too constricting to be loose and comfortable. Would the size 4 have fit me? Yes, but, again, not in a nice loose comfortable way that I wanted. Actually, I laughed when I unrolled them as they looked a bit comical with the wide legs and tiny looking waist (they come rolled up in plastic, which I hate that - it's why I am not always keen on ordering clothes from Amazon - no tags, in stinky plastic, ugh). Length: These come to just below my ankles. My legs are relatively longer than my upper body but I am still short so if you are short like I am, then these aren't going to look cropped. I didn't mind the length. Material: The material is soft and pliable. It's thick but not in a stiff way. It does have 55% cotton but I washed and dried mine on normal cycle so the water was warm and the dryer was very warm, before trying on, as I do everything because I'm just that way. I don't know if they shrank, but if so I believe it was minimal. These did have a chemical odor to them. Not overpowering but it was there, even somewhat after washing so I'll need to wash them twice. Another reason I hesitate ordering clothes from Amazon. Details: The front pockets are 'slit' pockets and I was surprised at how deep they are. The back pockets are the small 'patch' pockets. Other than that they are minimal in design, in a good way. Button and zipper fly that seem sturdy and well made. Stitching looks good. These are sort of style of 'mom jeans' but not really ...? I absolutely do not like the mom jean style and wish that had not come back but things will come and go - I am in my 60s so I've seen a lot of styles come and go! I do miss lower rise jeans as they fit with my shorter torso! But these in the size 6 don't constrict and I achieved that loose comfortable fit I wanted and they aren't too mom jean looking. Color: I almost ordered the darker pair but these light blue are nice too. A little retro. All in all they will be a good pair of jeans to wear when I want to feel comfortable. I wouldn't order another pair though.

Update: I probably could have kept the medium size as they stretch after wearing. Extremely comfortable, do wrinkle but not as much as 100% linen. I wore mine literally all day, by end of day they look very big on my body.I'm 5'1 145 lbs. Initially ordered a medium they fit just a tad tight, returned them and ordered a Large, perfecto! These are mostly linen, with some other material. It says 100% linen, but when ironing they smooth out very nicely, and when wearing they don't wrinkle as much as 100% linen. I love them. Ordered black, khaki and tan colors. I followed the instructions, wash cool, barely rinse then laid flat. Ironing was effortless. I would not fold over a hanger, instead, will hang by the waist area. Very well made, love the ankle gathering, and decent size front pockets, faux back pockets. I'm very pleased with this purchase.

UPDATE: I went with the size 6 and they fit like a dream. Still a bit baggy, but they’re the perfect length with shoes on (I’m 5’2”) and hug my butt well. So follow the sizing chart for your measurements then go two sizes down!! I have the issue of a small waist and big hips, so I rarely find jeans that fit. I have a 29” waist and 42” hips, but I read you should size down so I got a size 8. I’m wondering if I could size down one more to a 6, so I’m going to buy a size 6 and return whichever doesn’t fit! The size 8 fit well, but I think they could hug my butt a bit more. I’m a millennial trying to get away from skinny jeans, but these are definitely more baggy than they appeared on the model. I may get them hemmed since they’re too long on me (5’2”) and see if that helps at all. Overall, they’re comfy, just stretchy enough, and decent quality for $45

Update: Ordered 30L again and AGAIN got a 30x32. I give up… very disappointing. Don’t order these if you’re tall and wanting long jeans.Jeans fit well but noticed they felt short. I’m 5’10” and ordered a 30 Long. I was sent a 30 x 32. Checked the fit guide and says a 30L is 30 x 34! I’ll try ordering again an hopefully will get correct size.

Update: ordered another pair in size 4 and they fit better than any other skinny jeans I've ever bought..... but the color still isn't right.... it's like they are sending a completely different color and I really liked the lighter one in the options. They look lighter in my photos than in real life because I had to lighten the picture, since the room was kind of dim. The color is not as light (I ordered Capetown waterless) as the photos on here, but it's pretty much the same as the blue Laguna waterless. I think they are getting them mixed up or something. This is two pairs in the wrong color so far 🙄. The fit is good, but maybe runs a little big? I am 5'5" and 130lbs and the size 6 regular length was pretty long... Def doesn't seem like a 29" inseam... Seemed closer to 32". Ordered a pair in the 27" and the length is great, however I think I need to get a size 4 as this pair looks great but starts falling down a little when I'm walking around. LOVE that there is NO HUGE GAP IN THE BACK! Yay! Once I get the size perfect these will be the perfect pants.

Update: rhinestones on the back pockets all fell off with one wear. It’s like they weren’t even glued on! Just cheap. I didn’t like them on the back pockets anyways, but it’s the principle. I’m 5’4” 116lbs and pear shaped. I ordered size 1 short. I was looking for some cute mid rise boot cut jeans after seeing that the whole y2k trend is coming back into style. The ones I ordered came with more “bling” than shown in the picture. I don't personally like that look. The waist was gapping a bit on me. The short length was a bit short surprisingly, even though I have very short legs. I’m pretty sure a size down would be too small. I am giving the pants one more try I ordered the solid black color (that appears to have no bling at all) in regular length and may have to take in the waist. The light wash ones pictured I’m giving to a family member who likes them. Cute jeans through I hope the black ones I just ordered I like better.

Update: So I purchsed a 14 regular and a 14 long hoping those would give me the shrinkage room i need to get a nice 12. Huh! Jokes on me. The 14L, although much longer in length, fit a smidge SMALLER than the 12 and are tight in the knee! The 14R fit about the SAME as the 12 with this kicker-they about 2 inches shorter!!! So no shrinkage room. Maybe the 16?? I'll try it but do i the regular or the tall? Hmm, Can't win. Too bad as they are nice work slacks.Edit: they shrink, in and up, a lot. I lost probably 2 inches in both directions. Don't let the polyester feel of these slacks fool you. And read the washing instructions. And they still wrinkle. A lot. Which is too bad because they are a great work pant!!Slacks fit great, very comfortable. I did size up 1 size (normally a 12, bought a 14). A little snug in thigh but not uncomfortablely so. I'm 5'7 and hem sits just above my boot sole. Difficult to describe the material....maybe a little too polyester-ish and they wrinkle a LOT after one wear. Overall, a nice pair of slacks and i would buy again.

UPDATE! I purchased the Amanda white pair of jeans (size 18). I think this size hugs my body more and I like this style. I love the material, but be careful if you have a more curvy hourglass shape like I do. Pants fit very snug around thigh area, but is loose around waist area. I’m 5’7 and 235 lbs. I purchased size 14W plus size Long.

UPDATE! I'm now down around 100 lbs and am in a size 14w! I just ordered another pair in black and cannot wait to receive those too! Ladies, don't hesitate to purchase these jeans! They are comfy and make you feel SO GOOD ABOUT YOURSELF!!! 💖💖💖 I've recently lost almost 90 lbs and although the 18's I ordered appeared to fit fine they felt a little loose through my thighs after an hour so I returned them for a 16. I received those today and wowza! They fit like a glove! These have got to be the most flattering jeans I've ever purchased online! There's the PERFECT amount of flair and stretch and my hubby even loves them on me! For reference I am 5'6" and weigh 210 lbs. Part of me wants to order the size 12 or 14 too since I'm not at my goal weight yet! That's how much I love these jeans! Order them now! You won't regret it!

Update! These absolutely can be cut!! I cut off about three inches so I can wear them with sneakers or flats but they also look cute with heels. A rotary cutter and ruler made it super easy. I ordered them in the black too and they are SO FANTASTIC! They look just like the $100 Abercrombie ones! So happy! Original review: These pants are SO GOOD!!! I am shocked at how perfect these are! They fit perfectly and are so comfortable. They have SOME stretch to them so they are easy to wear for long periods. They will look better with a heel or even sneakers than a bootie, but I was too lazy to change my shoes and socks! :) Some thoughts below: - Follow the size chart for the best fit. I usually wear a womens 6 or 28" waist which can be a small or medium, based on the size chart, I went with the medium and they are SO perfect! I am 5'7" and 135lbs for reference. - It is a faux leather but it does not have that AWFUL fish smell that some do. The material doesn't look super fake either, it looks great. I was SHOCKED. - The inside almost has a lining to it, they are very comfortable and I imagine will keep you nice and warm - I love that it has belt loops and pockets! Neither are super large, but both appreciated. - No butt pockets but I am literally amazed at how good my butt looks in these! I was NOT expecting that! - The ends of the pant legs are unfinished so I THINK you could cut them if they were too long on you. I cannot say for sure as the length is great for me at 5'7" but it appears that you could (see photos) Overall, I am so in love with these pants, I already went back and ordered them in black. I am not kidding when I say I have ordered 10-15 pairs of faux leather pants to find THIS LOOK and I finally found it with these. I am so happy and I cannot recommend them enough!

Update... my coworkers loved my pants so much that they ordered the camo and black. You can see that thes pants work for all body shapes and heights. I'm 5'9 and weight 190 age 60 and I ordered an XL. I LOVE these pants, I could have gotten away with a large but I'm keeping this because they're very comfortable. As you can see they almost meet the floor on me. The denim is a medium weight and soft.

Updated review: I returned both sizes. They are just awful. The material seemed like it was nice but any friction, and I mean ANY, like just rubbing your hands on them, cause the fabric to deteriorate. I don't even want to imagine what would happen after washing. Also the butt..it's so sad. Just don't. The pants were much better quality than I expected. Heavy but soft, but not fragile material. I've lost a lot of weight and switch between anywhere from M,L,XL depending on the brand (not weight fluctuatation). I have larger breasts, wide hips and butt. Pros: good quality, comfortable, elastic band and button/zipper, big pockets, high waisted but could be worn lower Cons I saw another reviewer ask if these pants give you "sad butt" and I'm here to say and show the proof.... yes... yes they do give you sad butt. My butt is not happy 😆🤧 Length is kind of weird. I'm 5 foot 4" and they definitely don't fit me cropped even pulled up as high as they'll sit without a belt. They are hitting my ankles and top of feet in a way that evokes memories of childhood pants that had shrunk too short. Conclusion I'm going to order a pair in medium and update. I gave the pants a good review because it's not the pants fault I'm in between sizes and short. And I knew what I was getting into with the "sad butt" which hopefully will be remedied by a smaller size. We shall see.

Updated review.I ordered gray and dark blue. I recieved the dark blue and they are not dark blue at all. They look to me as if these are a purple. A light purple so I contacted amazon bc I really thought that they mistakenly sent the wrong color. So amazon sent me out another pair. I recieved them today and these are exactly the same color as the ones that I still have to return back to them. So just letting people know that they are not dark blue. Would be very cute if they got the dye correct and actually made them dark as shown in the picture. This is not a camera picture color difference. It's a huge difference in color and not even blue a all.. I posted 2 pics of what they say is dark blue. The gray ones are actually gray. Not dark gray but gray. I took 2 stars away bc of this and the annoyance plus having a return them back to UPS for refunds because of their mistake. I like these a lot. They are comfortable and easy to move around in bc they stretch . I've read reviews about broken pockets or rips in them. Mine came in just fine . I have no issues with the ones that I have received. They are not buttlifting at all . I can't remember if they were advertised as being so but they are not. I'm going to be using these as gym pants bc that is what they look and feel like . Oh and all pockets are real usable pockets . I'm 5"5 and these are just at my ankles so not tall girl friendly at all. They are not see thru either. I recommend these as workout / yoga pants or even if you have an operation like I have bc they're not tight around the tummy area .
